1Ôćĺ# MMS V1.0 GENESIS ÔÇö Master Orchestration Prompt
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ> **Purpose**: Consolidate 6 projects into one clean V1.0 baseline.
     4Ôćĺ> **Method**: Parallel extraction Ôćĺ Validation Ôćĺ Synthesis Ôćĺ Implementation.
     5Ôćĺ> **Resources**: UNLIMITED. Quality is the only metric.
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ---
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ## I. MISSION
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ**Consolidate all knowledge from 6 existing MMS projects into a single, clean V1.0 codebase.**
    12Ôćĺ
    13ÔćĺThe projects contain years of experimentation, multiple tech stack attempts, and distributed documentation. Your job is to:
    14Ôćĺ
    15Ôćĺ1. **EXTRACT** all valuable knowledge (domain, technical, UX)
    16Ôćĺ2. **VALIDATE** what actually works vs what's only documented
    17Ôćĺ3. **SYNTHESIZE** into one authoritative specification
    18Ôćĺ4. **IMPLEMENT** a clean baseline with new stack
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺ**Client**: Dubai Metro / Keolis MHI
    21Ôćĺ**Product**: Fleet maintenance planning visualization (128 trains, 2 depots, 12-year contract)
    22Ôćĺ**Core UX**: Semantic Zoom (Google Earth for time) ÔÇö pan through years, zoom into days
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ---
    25Ôćĺ
    26Ôćĺ## II. KNOWLEDGE SOURCES
    27Ôćĺ
    28Ôćĺ```
    29Ôćĺ~/antonio/mms/
    30ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ system/          # PRIMARY ÔÇö Production app (React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS)
    31ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ deck/            # POC ÔÇö deck.gl experiments, harness research
    32ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ MFC/             # Full-stack ÔÇö Next.js + Python OR-Tools solver
    33ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ projects/        # Historical ÔÇö Past attempts (4 subdirs)
    34ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ design/          # Visual ÔÇö Reference mockups (9 images)
    35ÔćĺÔööÔöÇÔöÇ system_started/  # Template ÔÇö Bootstrap structure
    36Ôćĺ```
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ**Key files to extract from**:
    39Ôćĺ- `system/docs/*.md` ÔÇö Domain specs, QA protocols, research
    40Ôćĺ- `system/apps/canvas/` ÔÇö Working LOD implementation
    41Ôćĺ- `system/.harness/` ÔÇö Decision history, learnings
    42Ôćĺ- `MFC/` ÔÇö Solver logic, mathematical formulas
    43Ôćĺ- `deck/` ÔÇö Alternative approaches, harness improvements
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ---
    46Ôćĺ
    47Ôćĺ## III. TARGET STATE
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ```
    50Ôćĺ~/antonio/mms/system/  (V1.0)
    51ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ apps/canvas/       # PatternFly v6 + Canvas (R3F or alternative)
    52ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ docs/SPECIFICATION.md  # Single source (~3000-4000 lines)
    53ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ .harness/          # Clean V1.0 feature tracking
    54ÔćĺÔööÔöÇÔöÇ CLAUDE.md          # Updated conventions
    55Ôćĺ```
    56Ôćĺ
    57Ôćĺ**Stack Migration**:
    58Ôćĺ| From | To |
    59Ôćĺ|------|-----|
    60Ôćĺ| BlueprintJS 6 | PatternFly v6 |
    61Ôćĺ| @zazcart/toolkit | @belt/tools |
    62Ôćĺ| packages/toolkit/ | DELETE (use npm) |
    63Ôćĺ
    64Ôćĺ---
    65Ôćĺ
    66Ôćĺ## IV. CORE HEURISTICS
    67Ôćĺ
    68Ôćĺ```
    69Ôćĺ1. TRUST NOTHING, VERIFY EVERYTHING
    70Ôćĺ   Documentation claims features work. Code may not run.
    71Ôćĺ   Categorize: VERIFIED (executed) | CLAIMED (documented) | DEPRECATED
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ2. PARALLEL WIDE, SEQUENTIAL DEEP
    74Ôćĺ   Extraction: Many agents, maximum coverage
    75Ôćĺ   Synthesis: One agent, full coherence
    76Ôćĺ
    77Ôćĺ3. DISK OVER MEMORY
    78Ôćĺ   Context rots. Write to _migration_memory/
    79Ôćĺ   Sub-agents return condensed summaries (1-2k tokens)
    80Ôćĺ
    81Ôćĺ4. ONE FEATURE, ONE SESSION
    82Ôćĺ   Incremental progress. Verify before proceeding.
    83Ôćĺ   Leave codebase mergeable.
    84Ôćĺ
    85Ôćĺ5. DECISIONS THROUGH DEBATE
    86Ôćĺ   Major choices: spawn SIMPLE vs COMPLETE vs PERF advocates
    87Ôćĺ   Synthesizer produces consensus with evidence
    88Ôćĺ```
    89Ôćĺ
    90Ôćĺ---
    91Ôćĺ
    92Ôćĺ## V. EXECUTION PHASES
    93Ôćĺ
    94Ôćĺ### PHASE 1: Territory Mapping
    95Ôćĺ**Agents**: 1
    96Ôćĺ**Output**: `_migration_memory/00_TERRITORY_MAP.md`
    97Ôćĺ
    98ÔćĺScan all 6 nodes. Count files, identify key assets, check git history. Do NOT read content yet.
    99Ôćĺ
   100Ôćĺ---
   101Ôćĺ
   102Ôćĺ### PHASE 2: Parallel Knowledge Extraction
   103Ôćĺ**Agents**: 6-8 parallel
   104Ôćĺ**Outputs**: `_migration_memory/01_*.md` through `08_*.md`
   105Ôćĺ
   106Ôćĺ| Agent | Target | Focus |
   107Ôćĺ|-------|--------|-------|
   108Ôćĺ| EXT-CANVAS | system/apps/canvas/ | What renders, what performs |
   109Ôćĺ| EXT-DOCS | system/docs/ | Domain knowledge, QA protocols |
   110Ôćĺ| EXT-HARNESS | system/.harness/ + git | Decision history, learnings |
   111Ôćĺ| EXT-MFC | MFC/ | Solver logic, math formulas |
   112Ôćĺ| EXT-DECK | deck/ | Alternative approaches |
   113Ôćĺ| EXT-PROJECTS | projects/ | Historical experiments |
   114Ôćĺ| EXT-DESIGN | design/ | Visual specifications |
   115Ôćĺ
   116Ôćĺ**HEVA Protocol Template** (for all extraction agents):
   117Ôćĺ```
   118ÔćĺFor each finding:
   119Ôćĺ1. HYPOTHESIS: "I believe [X] because..."
   120Ôćĺ2. EVIDENCE: "Found in [file:line] or [commit:hash]..."
   121Ôćĺ3. VERIFICATION: "Confirmed by [code executed / test passed / screenshot]..."
   122Ôćĺ4. ARTIFACT: Written to _migration_memory/[file].md
   123Ôćĺ
   124ÔćĺCategories:
   125Ôćĺ- VERIFIED: Code ran, test passed
   126Ôćĺ- CLAIMED: Documented but not verified
   127Ôćĺ- UNIQUE_KNOWLEDGE: Worth preserving
   128Ôćĺ- DEPRECATED: Do not carry forward
   129Ôćĺ
   130ÔćĺBE THOROUGH. Unlimited tokens. Miss nothing.
   131Ôćĺ```
   132Ôćĺ
   133Ôćĺ---
   134Ôćĺ
   135Ôćĺ### PHASE 3: Technology Research
   136Ôćĺ**Agents**: 3 parallel
   137Ôćĺ**Outputs**: `_migration_memory/10_*.md`
   138Ôćĺ
   139Ôćĺ| Agent | Focus |
   140Ôćĺ|-------|-------|
   141Ôćĺ| TECH-PF | PatternFly v6 ÔÇö components, integration, migration path |
   142Ôćĺ| TECH-BELT | @belt/tools ÔÇö API, patterns, migration from toolkit |
   143Ôćĺ| TECH-CANVAS | Canvas tech comparison ÔÇö R3F vs deck.gl vs alternatives |
   144Ôćĺ
   145Ôćĺ**Note**: Research happens HERE, not before. Agents discover what's needed.
   146Ôćĺ
   147Ôćĺ---
   148Ôćĺ
   149Ôćĺ### PHASE 4: Cross-Validation
   150Ôćĺ**Agents**: 3-4 parallel
   151Ôćĺ**Outputs**: `_migration_memory/20_*.md`
   152Ôćĺ
   153ÔćĺCross-reference extraction artifacts. Identify:
   154Ôćĺ- **CONFLICTS**: Different sources disagree
   155Ôćĺ- **GAPS**: Missing knowledge
   156Ôćĺ- **VERIFIED**: Multiple sources + code confirms
   157Ôćĺ
   158Ôćĺ---
   159Ôćĺ
   160Ôćĺ### PHASE 5: Decision Debates
   161Ôćĺ**Agents**: 4 (3 advocates + 1 synthesizer)
   162Ôćĺ**Output**: `_migration_memory/35_DECISIONS.md`
   163Ôćĺ
   164Ôćĺ**Topics**: Canvas technology, spec structure, V1.0 scope, PatternFly integration
   165Ôćĺ
   166Ôćĺ**Protocol**:
   167Ôćĺ1. Position papers (500 words each)
   168Ôćĺ2. Cross-examination
   169Ôćĺ3. Synthesis with evidence
   170Ôćĺ
   171Ôćĺ---
   172Ôćĺ
   173Ôćĺ### PHASE 6: Specification Writing
   174Ôćĺ**Agents**: 3 writers + 1 editor
   175Ôćĺ**Output**: `docs/SPECIFICATION.md`
   176Ôćĺ
   177ÔćĺWrite the FINAL spec. No migration notes. No alternatives. Only decided approaches.
   178Ôćĺ
   179ÔćĺInclude: Domain (Dubai Metro context), Architecture, UX, Math formulas, Demo strategy.
   180Ôćĺ
   181Ôćĺ---
   182Ôćĺ
   183Ôćĺ### PHASE 7: Repository Cleanup
   184Ôćĺ**Agent**: 1
   185Ôćĺ**Actions**:
   186Ôćĺ1. Backup branch
   187Ôćĺ2. Delete deprecated: `packages/`, old docs
   188Ôćĺ3. Update dependencies (PatternFly v6, @belt/tools)
   189Ôćĺ4. Archive old harness, create new
   190Ôćĺ5. Delete `_migration_memory/`
   191Ôćĺ6. Commit clean baseline
   192Ôćĺ
   193Ôćĺ---
   194Ôćĺ
   195Ôćĺ### PHASE 8: Implementation
   196Ôćĺ**Protocol**: Anthropic's two-agent architecture
   197Ôćĺ
   198Ôćĺ**Harness Structure** (JSON, not Markdown):
   199Ôćĺ```json
   200Ôćĺ// features.json
   201Ôćĺ{
   202Ôćĺ  "version": "1.0.0",
   203Ôćĺ  "features": [
   204Ôćĺ    {
   205Ôćĺ      "id": "F001",
   206Ôćĺ      "category": "foundation",
   207Ôćĺ      "description": "Vite + React 18 + PatternFly v6 scaffold",
   208Ôćĺ      "verification": ["npm run dev works", "PatternFly renders"],
   209Ôćĺ      "passes": false
   210Ôćĺ    }
   211Ôćĺ  ]
   212Ôćĺ}
   213Ôćĺ```
   214Ôćĺ
   215Ôćĺ**Feature Phases**:
   216Ôćĺ- **A (F001-F010)**: Canvas Foundation ÔÇö scaffold, Storybook, state, LOD layers, pan/zoom, 60fps
   217Ôćĺ- **B (F011-F015)**: UI Integration ÔÇö sidebar, timeline, minimap, drawer, keyboard
   218Ôćĺ- **C (F016-F020)**: Solver ÔÇö mock API, real solver, allocation, What-If, demo mode
   219Ôćĺ
   220Ôćĺ**Session Protocol** (every session):
   221Ôćĺ```
   222Ôćĺ1. RECOVER: cat progress.txt | tail -100 && git log --oneline -15
   223Ôćĺ2. SELECT: Find ONE incomplete feature in features.json
   224Ôćĺ3. VERIFY: Confirm previous feature still works
   225Ôćĺ4. IMPLEMENT: Code ONE feature only
   226Ôćĺ5. TEST: Visual + performance verification
   227Ôćĺ6. COMMIT: Atomic, descriptive message
   228Ôćĺ7. UPDATE: Mark passes:true, append to progress.txt
   229Ôćĺ8. STOP: Leave codebase mergeable
   230Ôćĺ```
   231Ôćĺ
   232Ôćĺ---
   233Ôćĺ
   234Ôćĺ## VI. PRESERVED KNOWLEDGE
   235Ôćĺ
   236Ôćĺ### Domain (Dubai Metro)
   237Ôćĺ- **Client**: Keolis MHI (Dubai Metro)
   238Ôćĺ- **Fleets**: Alstom (79, Red Line, Al Rashidiya depot) + Kinki Sharyo (49, Green Line, Jebel Ali depot)
   239Ôćĺ- **Total**: 128 trains, 9 tracks, 5,200 weekly man-hours
   240Ôćĺ- **Contract**: 12 years
   241Ôćĺ- **Maintenance**: A-Check (10k km, 4h) Ôćĺ B-Check (50k, 16h) Ôćĺ C-Check (200k, 72h) Ôćĺ D-Overhaul (800k, 480h)
   242Ôćĺ
   243Ôćĺ### Technical (from harness history)
   244Ôćĺ- **STATE-ARCH-001**: TypedArrays + SoA pattern for 60fps (GC is the bottleneck)
   245Ôćĺ- **TOOLKIT-PERF-001**: 7 GC optimization fixes documented
   246Ôćĺ- **LOD**: Macro (<0.5x) | Meso (0.5-2x) | Micro (>2x) with crossfade
   247Ôćĺ- **RBush**: O(log n) spatial index for viewport culling
   248Ôćĺ
   249Ôćĺ### KPI Formulas (PRESERVE)
   250Ôćĺ```javascript
   251ÔćĺkmVariance = (stddev(currentKms) / mean(currentKms)) * 100  // PRIMARY
   252Ôćĺavailability = (fitTrains / totalTrains) * 100
   253Ôćĺutilization = (scheduledTracks / availableTracks) * 100
   254ÔćĺtrainDelta = ((trainKm - fleetAvgKm) / fleetAvgKm) * 100
   255Ôćĺ```
   256Ôćĺ
   257Ôćĺ### Competitive Context
   258Ôćĺ| Competitor | Gap MMS Fills |
   259Ôćĺ|------------|---------------|
   260Ôćĺ| DELMIA Quintiq | Too complex, slow time-to-value |
   261Ôćĺ| Railnova | Basic optimization only |
   262Ôćĺ| SignatureRail | Black-box decisions, no transparency |
   263Ôćĺ
   264Ôćĺ### Demo Strategy (12 min)
   265Ôćĺ| Time | Moment | Wow Factor |
   266Ôćĺ|------|--------|------------|
   267Ôćĺ| 0:30-2:00 | Variance Discovery | "Your fleet is 18% out of balance" |
   268Ôćĺ| 2:00-5:00 | Justification Card | "3+ reasons per recommendation" |
   269Ôćĺ| 5:00-8:00 | What-If Simulation | "<10s response time" |
   270Ôćĺ| 8:00-11:00 | Map Context | "Geographic intelligence" |
   271Ôćĺ
   272Ôćĺ**Credibility**: Real Dubai depot names, realistic service codes (PM1-2024-0847), plausible km ranges
   273Ôćĺ
   274Ôćĺ---
   275Ôćĺ
   276Ôćĺ## VII. SPECIAL COMMANDS
   277Ôćĺ
   278Ôćĺ**ULTRATHINK**: Stop, review last 3 steps, verify no hallucination.
   279Ôćĺ
   280Ôćĺ**ESCALATE**: Only for business decisions, irreconcilable conflicts, or scope-changing discoveries.
   281Ôćĺ
   282Ôćĺ**CHECKPOINT**: Write state to disk, summarize, provide recovery instructions.
   283Ôćĺ
   284Ôćĺ---
   285Ôćĺ
   286Ôćĺ## VIII. EXECUTION
   287Ôćĺ
   288Ôćĺ**Session 1**: Phases 1-2 (Territory + Extraction)
   289Ôćĺ```
   290Ôćĺ[ ] mkdir -p ~/antonio/mms/system/_migration_memory
   291Ôćĺ[ ] Run Phase 1: Territory mapping (1 agent)
   292Ôćĺ[ ] Run Phase 2: Knowledge extraction (6-8 agents parallel)
   293Ôćĺ[ ] Verify all artifacts written to disk
   294Ôćĺ```
   295Ôćĺ
   296Ôćĺ**Session 2**: Phases 3-4 (Research + Validation)
   297Ôćĺ```
   298Ôćĺ[ ] Run Phase 3: Technology research (3 agents parallel)
   299Ôćĺ[ ] Run Phase 4: Cross-validation (3-4 agents parallel)
   300Ôćĺ[ ] Document conflicts and gaps
   301Ôćĺ```
   302Ôćĺ
   303Ôćĺ**Session 3**: Phase 5 (Debates)
   304Ôćĺ```
   305Ôćĺ[ ] Run Phase 5: Decision debates (4 agents)
   306Ôćĺ[ ] Produce consensus document (35_DECISIONS.md)
   307Ôćĺ[ ] All major decisions made, no open questions
   308Ôćĺ```
   309Ôćĺ
   310Ôćĺ**Session 4**: Phase 6 (Specification)
   311Ôćĺ```
   312Ôćĺ[ ] Run Phase 6: Specification writing (3 writers + 1 editor)
   313Ôćĺ[ ] Final docs/SPECIFICATION.md ready
   314Ôćĺ```
   315Ôćĺ
   316Ôćĺ**Session 5**: Phase 7 (Cleanup)
   317Ôćĺ```
   318Ôćĺ[ ] Backup branch created
   319Ôćĺ[ ] Deprecated folders deleted
   320Ôćĺ[ ] Dependencies updated
   321Ôćĺ[ ] _migration_memory/ deleted
   322Ôćĺ[ ] V1.0 baseline committed
   323Ôćĺ```
   324Ôćĺ
   325Ôćĺ**Session 6+**: Phase 8 (Implementation)
   326Ôćĺ```
   327Ôćĺ[ ] Follow session protocol (8 steps)
   328Ôćĺ[ ] One feature per session
   329Ôćĺ[ ] Verify before commit
   330Ôćĺ```
   331Ôćĺ
   332Ôćĺ---
   333Ôćĺ
   334Ôćĺ## START
   335Ôćĺ
   336Ôćĺ```bash
   337Ôćĺmkdir -p ~/antonio/mms/system/_migration_memory
   338Ôćĺ```
   339Ôćĺ
   340ÔćĺThen execute Phase 1. Do not ask questions. Execute.
   341Ôćĺ

# MMS V1.0 GENESIS ÔÇö Master Orchestration Prompt

> **Purpose**: Consolidate 6 projects into one clean V1.0 baseline.
> **Method**: Parallel extraction Ôćĺ Validation Ôćĺ Synthesis Ôćĺ Implementation.
> **Resources**: UNLIMITED. Quality is the only metric.

---

## I. MISSION

**Consolidate all knowledge from 6 existing MMS projects into a single, clean V1.0 codebase.**

The projects contain years of experimentation, multiple tech stack attempts, and distributed documentation. Your job is to:

1. **EXTRACT** all valuable knowledge (domain, technical, UX)
2. **VALIDATE** what actually works vs what's only documented
3. **SYNTHESIZE** into one authoritative specification
4. **IMPLEMENT** a clean baseline with new stack

**Client**: Dubai Metro / Keolis MHI
**Product**: Fleet maintenance planning visualization (128 trains, 2 depots, 12-year contract)
**Core UX**: Semantic Zoom (Google Earth for time) ÔÇö pan through years, zoom into days

---

## II. KNOWLEDGE SOURCES

```
~/antonio/mms/
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ system/          # PRIMARY ÔÇö Production app (React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS)
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ deck/            # POC ÔÇö deck.gl experiments, harness research
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ MFC/             # Full-stack ÔÇö Next.js + Python OR-Tools solver
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ projects/        # Historical ÔÇö Past attempts (4 subdirs)
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ design/          # Visual ÔÇö Reference mockups (9 images)
ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ system_started/  # Template ÔÇö Bootstrap structure
```

**Key files to extract from**:
- `system/docs/*.md` ÔÇö Domain specs, QA protocols, research
- `system/apps/canvas/` ÔÇö Working LOD implementation
- `system/.harness/` ÔÇö Decision history, learnings
- `MFC/` ÔÇö Solver logic, mathematical formulas
- `deck/` ÔÇö Alternative approaches, harness improvements

---

## III. TARGET STATE

```
~/antonio/mms/system/  (V1.0)
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ apps/canvas/       # PatternFly v6 + Canvas (R3F or alternative)
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ docs/SPECIFICATION.md  # Single source (~3000-4000 lines)
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ .harness/          # Clean V1.0 feature tracking
ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ CLAUDE.md          # Updated conventions
```

**Stack Migration**:
| From | To |
|------|-----|
| BlueprintJS 6 | PatternFly v6 |
| @zazcart/toolkit | @belt/tools |
| packages/toolkit/ | DELETE (use npm) |

---

## IV. CORE HEURISTICS

```
1. TRUST NOTHING, VERIFY EVERYTHING
   Documentation claims features work. Code may not run.
   Categorize: VERIFIED (executed) | CLAIMED (documented) | DEPRECATED

2. PARALLEL WIDE, SEQUENTIAL DEEP
   Extraction: Many agents, maximum coverage
   Synthesis: One agent, full coherence

3. DISK OVER MEMORY
   Context rots. Write to _migration_memory/
   Sub-agents return condensed summaries (1-2k tokens)

4. ONE FEATURE, ONE SESSION
   Incremental progress. Verify before proceeding.
   Leave codebase mergeable.

5. DECISIONS THROUGH DEBATE
   Major choices: spawn SIMPLE vs COMPLETE vs PERF advocates
   Synthesizer produces consensus with evidence
```

---

## V. EXECUTION PHASES

### PHASE 1: Territory Mapping
**Agents**: 1
**Output**: `_migration_memory/00_TERRITORY_MAP.md`

Scan all 6 nodes. Count files, identify key assets, check git history. Do NOT read content yet.

---

### PHASE 2: Parallel Knowledge Extraction
**Agents**: 6-8 parallel
**Outputs**: `_migration_memory/01_*.md` through `08_*.md`

| Agent | Target | Focus |
|-------|--------|-------|
| EXT-CANVAS | system/apps/canvas/ | What renders, what performs |
| EXT-DOCS | system/docs/ | Domain knowledge, QA protocols |
| EXT-HARNESS | system/.harness/ + git | Decision history, learnings |
| EXT-MFC | MFC/ | Solver logic, math formulas |
| EXT-DECK | deck/ | Alternative approaches |
| EXT-PROJECTS | projects/ | Historical experiments |
| EXT-DESIGN | design/ | Visual specifications |

**HEVA Protocol Template** (for all extraction agents):
```
For each finding:
1. HYPOTHESIS: "I believe [X] because..."
2. EVIDENCE: "Found in [file:line] or [commit:hash]..."
3. VERIFICATION: "Confirmed by [code executed / test passed / screenshot]..."
4. ARTIFACT: Written to _migration_memory/[file].md

Categories:
- VERIFIED: Code ran, test passed
- CLAIMED: Documented but not verified
- UNIQUE_KNOWLEDGE: Worth preserving
- DEPRECATED: Do not carry forward

BE THOROUGH. Unlimited tokens. Miss nothing.
```

---

### PHASE 3: Technology Research
**Agents**: 3 parallel
**Outputs**: `_migration_memory/10_*.md`

| Agent | Focus |
|-------|-------|
| TECH-PF | PatternFly v6 ÔÇö components, integration, migration path |
| TECH-BELT | @belt/tools ÔÇö API, patterns, migration from toolkit |
| TECH-CANVAS | Canvas tech comparison ÔÇö R3F vs deck.gl vs alternatives |

**Note**: Research happens HERE, not before. Agents discover what's needed.

---

### PHASE 4: Cross-Validation
**Agents**: 3-4 parallel
**Outputs**: `_migration_memory/20_*.md`

Cross-reference extraction artifacts. Identify:
- **CONFLICTS**: Different sources disagree
- **GAPS**: Missing knowledge
- **VERIFIED**: Multiple sources + code confirms

---

### PHASE 5: Decision Debates
**Agents**: 4 (3 advocates + 1 synthesizer)
**Output**: `_migration_memory/35_DECISIONS.md`

**Topics**: Canvas technology, spec structure, V1.0 scope, PatternFly integration

**Protocol**:
1. Position papers (500 words each)
2. Cross-examination
3. Synthesis with evidence

---

### PHASE 6: Specification Writing
**Agents**: 3 writers + 1 editor
**Output**: `docs/SPECIFICATION.md`

Write the FINAL spec. No migration notes. No alternatives. Only decided approaches.

Include: Domain (Dubai Metro context), Architecture, UX, Math formulas, Demo strategy.

---

### PHASE 7: Repository Cleanup
**Agent**: 1
**Actions**:
1. Backup branch
2. Delete deprecated: `packages/`, old docs
3. Update dependencies (PatternFly v6, @belt/tools)
4. Archive old harness, create new
5. Delete `_migration_memory/`
6. Commit clean baseline

---

### PHASE 8: Implementation
**Protocol**: Anthropic's two-agent architecture

**Harness Structure** (JSON, not Markdown):
```json
// features.json
{
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "F001",
      "category": "foundation",
      "description": "Vite + React 18 + PatternFly v6 scaffold",
      "verification": ["npm run dev works", "PatternFly renders"],
      "passes": false
    }
  ]
}
```

**Feature Phases**:
- **A (F001-F010)**: Canvas Foundation ÔÇö scaffold, Storybook, state, LOD layers, pan/zoom, 60fps
- **B (F011-F015)**: UI Integration ÔÇö sidebar, timeline, minimap, drawer, keyboard
- **C (F016-F020)**: Solver ÔÇö mock API, real solver, allocation, What-If, demo mode

**Session Protocol** (every session):
```
1. RECOVER: cat progress.txt | tail -100 && git log --oneline -15
2. SELECT: Find ONE incomplete feature in features.json
3. VERIFY: Confirm previous feature still works
4. IMPLEMENT: Code ONE feature only
5. TEST: Visual + performance verification
6. COMMIT: Atomic, descriptive message
7. UPDATE: Mark passes:true, append to progress.txt
8. STOP: Leave codebase mergeable
```

---

## VI. PRESERVED KNOWLEDGE

### Domain (Dubai Metro)
- **Client**: Keolis MHI (Dubai Metro)
- **Fleets**: Alstom (79, Red Line, Al Rashidiya depot) + Kinki Sharyo (49, Green Line, Jebel Ali depot)
- **Total**: 128 trains, 9 tracks, 5,200 weekly man-hours
- **Contract**: 12 years
- **Maintenance**: A-Check (10k km, 4h) Ôćĺ B-Check (50k, 16h) Ôćĺ C-Check (200k, 72h) Ôćĺ D-Overhaul (800k, 480h)

### Technical (from harness history)
- **STATE-ARCH-001**: TypedArrays + SoA pattern for 60fps (GC is the bottleneck)
- **TOOLKIT-PERF-001**: 7 GC optimization fixes documented
- **LOD**: Macro (<0.5x) | Meso (0.5-2x) | Micro (>2x) with crossfade
- **RBush**: O(log n) spatial index for viewport culling

### KPI Formulas (PRESERVE)
```javascript
kmVariance = (stddev(currentKms) / mean(currentKms)) * 100  // PRIMARY
availability = (fitTrains / totalTrains) * 100
utilization = (scheduledTracks / availableTracks) * 100
trainDelta = ((trainKm - fleetAvgKm) / fleetAvgKm) * 100
```

### Competitive Context
| Competitor | Gap MMS Fills |
|------------|---------------|
| DELMIA Quintiq | Too complex, slow time-to-value |
| Railnova | Basic optimization only |
| SignatureRail | Black-box decisions, no transparency |

### Demo Strategy (12 min)
| Time | Moment | Wow Factor |
|------|--------|------------|
| 0:30-2:00 | Variance Discovery | "Your fleet is 18% out of balance" |
| 2:00-5:00 | Justification Card | "3+ reasons per recommendation" |
| 5:00-8:00 | What-If Simulation | "<10s response time" |
| 8:00-11:00 | Map Context | "Geographic intelligence" |

**Credibility**: Real Dubai depot names, realistic service codes (PM1-2024-0847), plausible km ranges

---

## VII. SPECIAL COMMANDS

**ULTRATHINK**: Stop, review last 3 steps, verify no hallucination.

**ESCALATE**: Only for business decisions, irreconcilable conflicts, or scope-changing discoveries.

**CHECKPOINT**: Write state to disk, summarize, provide recovery instructions.

---

## VIII. EXECUTION

**Session 1**: Phases 1-2 (Territory + Extraction)
```
[ ] mkdir -p ~/antonio/mms/system/_migration_memory
[ ] Run Phase 1: Territory mapping (1 agent)
[ ] Run Phase 2: Knowledge extraction (6-8 agents parallel)
[ ] Verify all artifacts written to disk
```

**Session 2**: Phases 3-4 (Research + Validation)
```
[ ] Run Phase 3: Technology research (3 agents parallel)
[ ] Run Phase 4: Cross-validation (3-4 agents parallel)
[ ] Document conflicts and gaps
```

**Session 3**: Phase 5 (Debates)
```
[ ] Run Phase 5: Decision debates (4 agents)
[ ] Produce consensus document (35_DECISIONS.md)
[ ] All major decisions made, no open questions
```

**Session 4**: Phase 6 (Specification)
```
[ ] Run Phase 6: Specification writing (3 writers + 1 editor)
[ ] Final docs/SPECIFICATION.md ready
```

**Session 5**: Phase 7 (Cleanup)
```
[ ] Backup branch created
[ ] Deprecated folders deleted
[ ] Dependencies updated
[ ] _migration_memory/ deleted
[ ] V1.0 baseline committed
```

**Session 6+**: Phase 8 (Implementation)
```
[ ] Follow session protocol (8 steps)
[ ] One feature per session
[ ] Verify before commit
```

---

## START

```bash
mkdir -p ~/antonio/mms/system/_migration_memory
```

Then execute Phase 1. Do not ask questions. Execute.

   300Ôćĺ```
   301ÔćĺSPECIFICATION WRITER INSTRUCTION:
   302ÔćĺYou are writing the definitive MMS V1.0 specification.
   303Ôćĺ
   304ÔćĺINPUTS (read first):
   305Ôćĺ- All files in _migration_memory/
   306Ôćĺ- Decision document from Phase 5 (35_decisions.md)
   307Ôćĺ
   308ÔćĺRULES:
   309Ôćĺ1. This is a FINAL document, not a migration plan
   310Ôćĺ2. No historical notes ("we used to...", "previously...")
   311Ôćĺ3. No alternatives ("you could also...") ÔÇö only the decided approach
   312Ôćĺ4. Include ALL validated mathematical formulas
   313Ôćĺ5. Include ALL domain knowledge (ECM, RAMS, EN 15380)
   314Ôćĺ6. Include PatternFly component mappings with code examples
   315Ôćĺ7. Include @belt/tools patterns with code examples
   316Ôćĺ8. Include demo strategy and "wow moments"
   317Ôćĺ9. Write in clear, technical English
   318Ôćĺ10. Target ~3000-4000 lines total
   319Ôćĺ
   320ÔćĺWrite your assigned sections to docs/SPECIFICATION.md
   321Ôćĺ```
   322Ôćĺ
   323Ôćĺ---
   324Ôćĺ
   325Ôćĺ### Phase 7: Repository Cleanup
   326Ôćĺ
   327Ôćĺ**Objective**: Transform codebase into V1.0 clean baseline.
   328Ôćĺ**Agent**: 1 (requires careful sequencing)
   329Ôćĺ
   330Ôćĺ```bash
   331Ôćĺ# 1. Create backup branch
   332Ôćĺgit checkout -b backup/pre-v1-$(date +%Y%m%d)
   333Ôćĺgit push origin backup/pre-v1-$(date +%Y%m%d)
   334Ôćĺ
   335Ôćĺ# 2. Return to main
   336Ôćĺgit checkout main
   337Ôćĺ
   338Ôćĺ# 3. Remove deprecated directories
   339Ôćĺrm -rf packages/
   340Ôćĺrm -rf agent_docs/
   341Ôćĺ
   342Ôćĺ# 4. Clean docs (preserve only new SPECIFICATION.md)
   343Ôćĺfind docs/ -name "*.md" ! -name "SPECIFICATION.md" -delete
   344Ôćĺ
   345Ôćĺ# 5. Update dependencies in package.json
   346Ôćĺ# - Remove @zazcart/toolkit references
   347Ôćĺ# - Add @belt/tools
   348Ôćĺ# - Add @patternfly/react-core@6.x
   349Ôćĺ# - Add @patternfly/react-table@6.x
   350Ôćĺ# - Add @patternfly/react-topology@6.x
   351Ôćĺ
   352Ôćĺ# 6. Archive old harness, create new
   353Ôćĺmkdir -p .harness/archive/pre-v1
   354Ôćĺmv .harness/*.txt .harness/archive/pre-v1/
   355Ôćĺmv .harness/*.json .harness/archive/pre-v1/
   356Ôćĺ
   357Ôćĺ# 7. Create new harness files (see Phase 8 templates)
   358Ôćĺ
   359Ôćĺ# 8. Update CLAUDE.md for V1.0
   360Ôćĺ
   361Ôćĺ# 9. Delete migration memory (git preserves history)
   362Ôćĺrm -rf _migration_memory/
   363Ôćĺ
   364Ôćĺ# 10. Commit clean baseline
   365Ôćĺgit add -A
   366Ôćĺgit commit -m "chore: MMS V1.0 genesis - clean baseline
   367Ôćĺ
   368Ôćĺ- Removed deprecated packages/ directory
   369Ôćĺ- Consolidated documentation to SPECIFICATION.md
   370Ôćĺ- Updated dependencies for PatternFly v6 + @belt/tools
   371Ôćĺ- Reset harness for V1.0 feature development
   372Ôćĺ- Archived pre-V1 harness history"
   373Ôćĺ```
   374Ôćĺ
   375Ôćĺ---
   376Ôćĺ
   377Ôćĺ### Phase 8: Implementation
   378Ôćĺ
   379Ôćĺ**Objective**: Build V1.0 features following harness protocol.
   380Ôćĺ**Pattern**: Anthropic's two-agent architecture (Initializer + Coding Agent)
   381Ôćĺ
   382Ôćĺ#### 8.1 Harness Files (JSON for structure integrity)
   383Ôćĺ
   384Ôćĺ**features.json** (created by Initializer Agent):
   385Ôćĺ```json
   386Ôćĺ{
   387Ôćĺ  "version": "1.0.0",
   388Ôćĺ  "created": "2025-01-XX",
   389Ôćĺ  "features": [
   390Ôćĺ    {
   391Ôćĺ      "id": "F001",
   392Ôćĺ      "category": "foundation",
   393Ôćĺ      "description": "Vite + React 18 + PatternFly v6 scaffold",
   394Ôćĺ      "verification": [
   395Ôćĺ        "npm run dev starts without errors",
   396Ôćĺ        "PatternFly components render correctly",
   397Ôćĺ        "No console errors in browser"
   398Ôćĺ      ],
   399Ôćĺ      "passes": false,
   400Ôćĺ      "priority": 1
   401Ôćĺ    },
   402Ôćĺ    {
   403Ôćĺ      "id": "F002",
   404Ôćĺ      "category": "foundation",
   405Ôćĺ      "description": "Storybook 8 setup with PatternFly theme",
   406Ôćĺ      "verification": [
   407Ôćĺ        "npm run storybook starts",
   408Ôćĺ        "Sample story renders PatternFly button"
   409Ôćĺ      ],
   410Ôćĺ      "passes": false,
   411Ôćĺ      "priority": 2
   412Ôćĺ    },
   413Ôćĺ    {
   414Ôćĺ      "id": "F003",
   415Ôćĺ      "category": "foundation",
   416Ôćĺ      "description": "@belt/tools integration",
   417Ôćĺ      "verification": [
   418Ôćĺ        "createSlice works in test file",
   419Ôćĺ        "State updates trigger re-renders"
   420Ôćĺ      ],
   421Ôćĺ      "passes": false,
   422Ôćĺ      "priority": 3
   423Ôćĺ    }
   424Ôćĺ  ]
   425Ôćĺ}
   426Ôćĺ```
   427Ôćĺ
   428Ôćĺ**Feature List (complete)**:
   429Ôćĺ```
   430ÔćĺPHASE A: Canvas Foundation
   431ÔćĺF001: Vite + React 18 + PatternFly v6 scaffold
   432ÔćĺF002: Storybook 8 setup
   433ÔćĺF003: @belt/tools integration
   434ÔćĺF004: Canvas container component (accepts data props)
   435ÔćĺF005: Macro LOD layer (heatmap, single draw call)
   436ÔćĺF006: Meso LOD layer (task blocks, InstancedMesh)
   437ÔćĺF007: Micro LOD layer (detail cards, HTML overlay)
   438ÔćĺF008: LOD transitions (crossfade 0.1 zoom units)
   439ÔćĺF009: Pan/Zoom controls
   440ÔćĺF010: 60fps verification (performance test)
   441Ôćĺ
   442ÔćĺPHASE B: UI Integration
   443ÔćĺF011: Train sidebar (PatternFly Table)
   444ÔćĺF012: Timeline header
   445ÔćĺF013: Minimap
   446ÔćĺF014: Task details (PatternFly Drawer)
   447ÔćĺF015: Keyboard navigation
   448Ôćĺ
   449ÔćĺPHASE C: Solver Integration
   450ÔćĺF016: Mock API with realistic data

   454ÔćĺF020: Demo mode with scripted scenarios
   455Ôćĺ```
   456Ôćĺ
   457Ôćĺ#### 8.2 Session Protocol (Coding Agent)
   458Ôćĺ
   459Ôćĺ```
   460ÔćĺEVERY SESSION:
   461Ôćĺ
   462Ôćĺ1. RECOVER CONTEXT
   463Ôćĺ   cat .harness/progress.txt | tail -100
   464Ôćĺ   git log --oneline -15
   465Ôćĺ   cat .harness/features.json | jq '.features[] | select(.passes==false) | .id' | head -1
   466Ôćĺ
   467Ôćĺ2. VERIFY PREVIOUS
   468Ôćĺ   Run the app
   469Ôćĺ   Confirm last completed feature still works
   470Ôćĺ   If broken: fix before proceeding
   471Ôćĺ
   472Ôćĺ3. SELECT ONE FEATURE
   473Ôćĺ   Pick the lowest-priority incomplete feature
   474Ôćĺ   Read its verification criteria
   475Ôćĺ
   476Ôćĺ4. IMPLEMENT
   477Ôćĺ   Write code for ONE feature only
   478Ôćĺ   Follow patterns in SPECIFICATION.md
   479Ôćĺ   Use PatternFly components, @belt/tools state
   480Ôćĺ
   481Ôćĺ5. VERIFY
   482Ôćĺ   Run all verification steps for the feature
   483Ôćĺ   Take screenshot if visual
   484Ôćĺ   Run tests if applicable
   485Ôćĺ
   486Ôćĺ6. COMMIT
   487Ôćĺ   git add -A
   488Ôćĺ   git commit -m "feat(F0XX): [description]
   489Ôćĺ
   490Ôćĺ   Verification:
   491Ôćĺ   - [x] Step 1 passed
   492Ôćĺ   - [x] Step 2 passed"
   493Ôćĺ
   494Ôćĺ7. UPDATE HARNESS
   495Ôćĺ   Mark feature as passes: true in features.json
   496Ôćĺ   Append session summary to progress.txt:
   497Ôćĺ   - What was accomplished
   498Ôćĺ   - Decisions made and rationale
   499Ôćĺ   - What should happen next
   500Ôćĺ   - Any blockers or concerns
   501Ôćĺ
   502Ôćĺ8. STOP
   503Ôćĺ   Leave codebase in clean, mergeable state
   504Ôćĺ   Do not start another feature
   505Ôćĺ```
   506Ôćĺ
   507Ôćĺ---
   508Ôćĺ
   509Ôćĺ## PART V: PRESERVED KNOWLEDGE
   510Ôćĺ
   511Ôćĺ### 5.1 Critical Technical Learnings
   512Ôćĺ
   513Ôćĺ**STATE-ARCH-001: TypedArrays + SoA for 60fps**
   514Ôćĺ```javascript
   515Ôćĺ// Structure of Arrays pattern
   516Ôćĺconst trainData = {
   517Ôćĺ  ids: new Uint32Array(MAX_TRAINS),
   518Ôćĺ  x: new Float32Array(MAX_TRAINS),
   519Ôćĺ  y: new Float32Array(MAX_TRAINS),
   520Ôćĺ  km: new Float32Array(MAX_TRAINS),
   521Ôćĺ  status: new Uint8Array(MAX_TRAINS),
   522Ôćĺ  count: 0
   523Ôćĺ};
   524Ôćĺ
   525Ôćĺ// Update without GC pressure
   526Ôćĺfunction updateTrain(index, x, y, km) {
   527Ôćĺ  trainData.x[index] = x;
   528Ôćĺ  trainData.y[index] = y;
   529Ôćĺ  trainData.km[index] = km;
   530Ôćĺ  // No object allocation = no GC = stable 60fps
   531Ôćĺ}
   532Ôćĺ```
   533Ôćĺ
   534Ôćĺ**TOOLKIT-PERF-001: 7 GC Optimization Fixes**
   535Ôćĺ1. Copy array before iteration (unsafe unsubscribe)
   536Ôćĺ2. Skip validation in production (hot path)
   537Ôćĺ3. Object.assign instead of reduceObject (O(N) vs O(N┬▓))
   538Ôćĺ4. `trackMetadata: false` option (eliminate GC thrashing)
   539Ôćĺ5. Clean object structure (prevent V8 de-optimization)
   540Ôćĺ6. Direct assignment (avoid double proxying)
   541Ôćĺ7. Safe error handling in devLog
   542Ôćĺ
   543Ôćĺ**LOD System Thresholds**:
   544Ôćĺ```javascript
   545Ôćĺconst LOD_THRESHOLDS = {
   546Ôćĺ  MACRO: { max: 0.5 },   // zoom < 0.5x: Aggregated heatmap
   547Ôćĺ  MESO: { min: 0.5, max: 2.0 },  // 0.5x-2x: Task blocks
   548Ôćĺ  MICRO: { min: 2.0 }    // zoom > 2x: Detail cards
   549Ôćĺ};
   550Ôćĺ
   551Ôćĺconst CROSSFADE_WIDTH = 0.1; // Smooth transition zone
   552Ôćĺ```
   553Ôćĺ
   554Ôćĺ**RBush Spatial Index**: O(log n) viewport culling
   555Ôćĺ```javascript
   556Ôćĺimport RBush from 'rbush';
   557Ôćĺconst tree = new RBush();
   558Ôćĺtree.load(tasks.map(t => ({
   559Ôćĺ  minX: t.x, minY: t.y,
   560Ôćĺ  maxX: t.x + t.width, maxY: t.y + t.height,
   561Ôćĺ  task: t
   562Ôćĺ})));
   563Ôćĺconst visible = tree.search(viewport);
   564Ôćĺ```
   565Ôćĺ
   566Ôćĺ### 5.2 Domain Knowledge
   567Ôćĺ
   568Ôćĺ**ECM 2019/779 Functions**:
   569Ôćĺ| Function | Name | MMS Feature |
   570Ôćĺ|----------|------|-------------|
   571Ôćĺ| F1 | Management | Audit logs, compliance dashboard |
   572Ôćĺ| F2 | Maintenance Development | Rule configuration, threshold settings |
   573Ôćĺ| F3 | Fleet Maintenance Management | Km balancing, scheduling optimization |
   574Ôćĺ| F4 | Maintenance Delivery | Work order tracking, completion status |
   575Ôćĺ
   576Ôćĺ**RAMS Metrics (EN 50126-1)**:
   577Ôćĺ```javascript
   578Ôćĺconst RAMS = {
   579Ôćĺ  reliability: {
   580Ôćĺ    mtbf: totalOperatingHours / failureCount,
   581Ôćĺ    failureRate: failureCount / totalOperatingHours
   582Ôćĺ  },
   583Ôćĺ  availability: {
   584Ôćĺ    operational: (totalTime - downtimeHours) / totalTime * 100,
   585Ôćĺ    inherent: mtbf / (mtbf + mttr)
   586Ôćĺ  },
   587Ôćĺ  maintainability: {
   588Ôćĺ    mttr: totalRepairHours / repairCount,
   589Ôćĺ    completionRate: completedWorkOrders / totalWorkOrders * 100
   590Ôćĺ  },
   591Ôćĺ  safety: {
   592Ôćĺ    hazardRate: safetyIncidents / operatingHours,
   593Ôćĺ    severityIndex: weightedSeveritySum / incidentCount
   594Ôćĺ  }
   595Ôćĺ};
   596Ôćĺ```
   597Ôćĺ
   598Ôćĺ**EN 15380 Terminology Mapping**:
   599Ôćĺ| Common Term | EN 15380 Code | Interval | Duration |
   600Ôćĺ|-------------|---------------|----------|----------|
   601Ôćĺ| A-Check | PM1 | 15,000 km | 15-60 min |
   602Ôćĺ| B-Check | PM2 | 45,000 km | 4-16 hours |
   603Ôćĺ| C-Check | HM | 90,000 km | 1-3 days |
   604Ôćĺ| D-Overhaul | OVH | 500,000-800,000 km | 2-6 weeks |
   605Ôćĺ
   606Ôćĺ**KPI Formulas**:
   607Ôćĺ```javascript
   608Ôćĺ// Km Variance (coefficient of variation) - PRIMARY METRIC
   609Ôćĺconst kmVariance = (standardDeviation(currentKms) / mean(currentKms)) * 100;
   610Ôćĺ
   611Ôćĺ// Fleet Availability
   612Ôćĺconst availability = (fitTrains / totalTrains) * 100;
   613Ôćĺ
   614Ôćĺ// Depot Utilization
   615Ôćĺconst utilization = (scheduledTracks / availableTracks) * 100;
   616Ôćĺ
   617Ôćĺ// Train Delta (individual vs fleet average)
   618Ôćĺconst trainDelta = ((trainKm - fleetAvgKm) / fleetAvgKm) * 100;
   619Ôćĺ
   620Ôćĺ// Maintenance Window Efficiency
   621Ôćĺconst windowEfficiency = actualWorkHours / scheduledWindowHours * 100;
   622Ôćĺ```
   623Ôćĺ
   624Ôćĺ### 5.3 Competitive Analysis
   625Ôćĺ
   626Ôćĺ| Competitor | Focus | Strength | MMS Opportunity |
   627Ôćĺ|------------|-------|----------|-----------------|
   628Ôćĺ| DELMIA Quintiq | Large-scale optimization | Powerful solver | Simpler implementation, faster time-to-value |
   629Ôćĺ| Tracsis | UK rail operations | Strong timetabling | Less maintenance focus |
   630Ôćĺ| Railnova | Fleet telematics | Real-time data, 96% ML accuracy | Basic optimization only |
   631Ôćĺ| SignatureRail | Fleet maintenance | Clean UI, good workflow | No optimization, black-box decisions |
   632Ôćĺ| ZEDAS | ECM compliance | AR maintenance assistance | Complex, enterprise-only |
   633Ôćĺ
   634Ôćĺ**MMS Differentiators**:
   635Ôćĺ1. Km Balancing as primary objective (competitors treat as secondary)
   636Ôćĺ2. Transparent justifications (3+ reasons per recommendation)
   637Ôćĺ3. What-If simulation accessible to non-technical users
   638Ôćĺ4. Unified maintenance Ôćö operations interface
   639Ôćĺ
   640Ôćĺ### 5.4 Demo Strategy
   641Ôćĺ
   642Ôćĺ**Timeline**: 12 minutes total
   643Ôćĺ
   644Ôćĺ| Time | Moment | Action | Wow Factor |
   645Ôćĺ|------|--------|--------|------------|
   646Ôćĺ| 0:30-2:00 | Variance Discovery | Dashboard shows 18% km variance | "Your fleet is out of balance" |
   647Ôćĺ| 2:00-5:00 | Justification Card | Click recommendation, see 3+ reasons | "Transparent AI decisions" |
   648Ôćĺ| 5:00-8:00 | What-If Simulation | Drag task, see instant projection | "<10s response time" |
   649Ôćĺ| 8:00-11:00 | Map Context | Toggle map view, see km delta overlay | "Geographic intelligence" |
   650Ôćĺ| 11:00-12:00 | Close | Summary metrics, next steps | "Reduce variance to 8%" |
   651Ôćĺ
   652Ôćĺ**Credibility Elements**:
   653Ôćĺ- EN 15380 terminology throughout
   654Ôćĺ- Real Portuguese depot names (Entroncamento, Campolide, Santa Apol├│nia)
   655Ôćĺ- Realistic service codes (PM1-2024-0847)
   656Ôćĺ- Plausible km ranges (45,000 - 180,000 km)
   657Ôćĺ
   658Ôćĺ---
   659Ôćĺ
   660Ôćĺ## PART VI: SPECIAL COMMANDS
   661Ôćĺ
   662Ôćĺ### ULTRATHINK
   663Ôćĺ
   664ÔćĺWhen user says "ULTRATHINK":
   665Ôćĺ1. Stop immediately
   666Ôćĺ2. Review last 3 steps recursively
   667Ôćĺ3. Check for hallucination or assumption
   668Ôćĺ4. Document verification process
   669Ôćĺ5. Only continue after explicit confirmation
   670Ôćĺ
   671Ôćĺ### ESCALATE
   672Ôćĺ
   673ÔćĺEscalate to user ONLY if:
   674Ôćĺ- Decision requires business input (not technical)
   675Ôćĺ- Irreconcilable conflict between sources
   676Ôćĺ- Blocker preventing progress
   677Ôćĺ- Discovery that significantly changes scope
   678Ôćĺ
   679ÔćĺFor everything else: research, decide, document rationale.
   680Ôćĺ
   681Ôćĺ### CHECKPOINT
   682Ôćĺ
   683ÔćĺWhen user says "CHECKPOINT":
   684Ôćĺ1. Write current state to _migration_memory/CHECKPOINT_[timestamp].md
   685Ôćĺ2. Summarize: completed phases, pending phases, key decisions, blockers
   686Ôćĺ3. Commit all changes with checkpoint message
   687Ôćĺ4. Provide recovery instructions for next session
   688Ôćĺ
   689Ôćĺ---
   690Ôćĺ
   691Ôćĺ## PART VII: EXECUTION CHECKLIST
   692Ôćĺ
   693Ôćĺ```
   694ÔćĺSESSION 1: Phases 1-2 (Territory + Extraction)
   695Ôćĺ[ ] mkdir -p ~/antonio/mms/system/_migration_memory
   696Ôćĺ[ ] Run Phase 1: Territory mapping (1 agent)
   697Ôćĺ[ ] Run Phase 2: Knowledge extraction (8-10 agents parallel)
   698Ôćĺ[ ] Verify all artifacts written to disk
   699Ôćĺ[ ] Review for completeness before proceeding
   700Ôćĺ
   701ÔćĺSESSION 2: Phases 3-4 (Research + Validation)
   702Ôćĺ[ ] Run Phase 3: Technology research (3 agents parallel)
   703Ôćĺ[ ] Run Phase 4: Cross-validation (4 agents parallel)
   704Ôćĺ[ ] Review conflicts and gaps
   705Ôćĺ[ ] Document unresolved issues
   706Ôćĺ
   707ÔćĺSESSION 3: Phase 5 (Debates)
   708Ôćĺ[ ] Run Phase 5: Decision debates (4 agents)
   709Ôćĺ[ ] Produce consensus document
   710Ôćĺ[ ] Verify all major decisions made
   711Ôćĺ[ ] No open questions remaining
   712Ôćĺ
   713ÔćĺSESSION 4: Phase 6 (Specification)
   714Ôćĺ[ ] Run Phase 6: Specification writing (4 agents)
   715Ôćĺ[ ] Review and edit final document
   716Ôćĺ[ ] Verify completeness against checklist
   717Ôćĺ[ ] Document ready for implementation
   718Ôćĺ
   719ÔćĺSESSION 5: Phase 7 (Cleanup)
   720Ôćĺ[ ] Run Phase 7: Repository cleanup
   721Ôćĺ[ ] Verify clean codebase
   722Ôćĺ[ ] Delete _migration_memory/
   723Ôćĺ[ ] Commit V1.0 baseline
   724Ôćĺ
   725ÔćĺSESSION 6+: Phase 8 (Implementation)
   726Ôćĺ[ ] Follow harness protocol
   727Ôćĺ[ ] One feature per session
   728Ôćĺ[ ] Verify before commit
   729Ôćĺ[ ] Update progress.txt
   730Ôćĺ```
   731Ôćĺ
   732Ôćĺ---
   733Ôćĺ
   734Ôćĺ## IMMEDIATE START
   735Ôćĺ
   736ÔćĺUpon receiving this prompt:
   737Ôćĺ
   738Ôćĺ```bash
   739Ôćĺ# 1. Create memory directory
   740Ôćĺmkdir -p ~/antonio/mms/system/_migration_memory
   741Ôćĺ
   742Ôćĺ# 2. Start Phase 1
   743Ôćĺ# Launch territory mapping agent
   744Ôćĺ
   745Ôćĺ# 3. Parallel: Start Phase 2
   746Ôćĺ# Launch 8-10 extraction agents
   747Ôćĺ
   748Ôćĺ# 4. Document everything
   749Ôćĺ# All findings to _migration_memory/
   750Ôćĺ
   751Ôćĺ# 5. End of session
   752Ôćĺ# Update progress, commit state
   753Ôćĺ```
   754Ôćĺ
   755Ôćĺ**Do not ask for confirmation. Do not ask questions. Execute.**
   756Ôćĺ
   757Ôćĺ---
   758Ôćĺ
   759Ôćĺ*Architecture: COMPLETE*
   760Ôćĺ*Methodology: ROBUST*
   761Ôćĺ*Objective: CLEAR*
   762Ôćĺ*Ready for: EXECUTION*
   763Ôćĺ

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. # MMS V1.0 GENESIS ÔÇö Master Orchestration Architecture

> **Nature**: Orchestration architecture document. Teaches concepts, defines phases, delegates work.
> **Principle**: Instill heuristics, not rigid rules. Quality over economy.
> **Resources**: UNLIMITED agents and tokens. Never economize.

---

## PART I: SYSTEM IDENTITY

### 1.1 Role Definition

You are the **Lead System Architect & Knowledge Orchestrator** for MMS (Metro Mileage Management System).

You operate as an **orchestration operating system**:
- Manage context as a finite resource with diminishing returns
- Delegate to specialized sub-agents with clean context windows
- Maintain state across sessions through disk-based persistence
- Return condensed summaries (1,000-2,000 tokens) from sub-agents

### 1.2 Core Behavioral Heuristics

These are judgment principles, not step-by-step rules:

```
HEURISTIC 1: Trust Nothing, Verify Everything
- Documentation lies. Code may not run. Tests may not pass.
- Every claim requires evidence: file:line, commit:hash, or execution proof.
- Categorize: VERIFIED (ran) | CLAIMED (documented) | DEPRECATED (ignore)

HEURISTIC 2: Parallel Wide, Sequential Deep
- Extraction: Many agents, maximum coverage, loose coordination
- Validation: Cross-reference, resolve conflicts, identify gaps
- Synthesis: Single agent, full context, coherent output

HEURISTIC 3: Disk Over Memory
- Context windows rot. Write everything to _migration_memory/

## PART V: PRESERVED KNOWLEDGE

### 5.1 Critical Technical Learnings

**STATE-ARCH-001: TypedArrays + SoA for 60fps**
```javascript
// Structure of Arrays pattern
const trainData = {
  ids: new Uint32Array(MAX_TRAINS),
  x: new Float32Array(MAX_TRAINS),
  y: new Float32Array(MAX_TRAINS),
  km: new Float32Array(MAX_TRAINS),
  status: new Uint8Array(MAX_TRAINS),
  count: 0
};

// Update without GC pressure
function updateTrain(index, x, y, km) {
  trainData.x[index] = x;
  trainData.y[index] = y;
  trainData.km[index] = km;
  // No object allocation = no GC = stable 60fps
}
```

**TOOLKIT-PERF-001: 7 GC Optimization Fixes**
1. Copy array before iteration (unsafe unsubscribe)
2. Skip validation in production (hot path)
3. Object.assign instead of reduceObject (O(N) vs O(N┬▓))
4. `trackMetadata: false` option (eliminate GC thrashing)
5. Clean object structure (prevent V8 de-optimization)
6. Direct assignment (avoid double proxying)
7. Safe error handling in devLog

**LOD System Thresholds**:
```javascript
const LOD_THRESHOLDS = {
  MACRO: { max: 0.5 },   // zoom < 0.5x: Aggregated heatmap
  MESO: { min: 0.5, max: 2.0 },  // 0.5x-2x: Task blocks
  MICRO: { min: 2.0 }    // zoom > 2x: Detail cards
};

const CROSSFADE_WIDTH = 0.1; // Smooth transition zone
```

**RBush Spatial Index**: O(log n) viewport culling
```javascript
import RBush from 'rbush';
const tree = new RBush();
tree.load(tasks.map(t => ({
  minX: t.x, minY: t.y,
  maxX: t.x + t.width, maxY: t.y + t.height,
  task: t
})));
const visible = tree.search(viewport);
```

**RAMS Metrics (EN 50126-1)**:
```javascript
const RAMS = {
  reliability: {
    mtbf: totalOperatingHours / failureCount,
    failureRate: failureCount / totalOperatingHours
  },
  availability: {
    operational: (totalTime - downtimeHours) / totalTime * 100,
    inherent: mtbf / (mtbf + mttr)
  },
  maintainability: {
    mttr: totalRepairHours / repairCount,
    completionRate: completedWorkOrders / totalWorkOrders * 100
  },
  safety: {
    hazardRate: safetyIncidents / operatingHours,
    severityIndex: weightedSeveritySum / incidentCount
  }
};
```

**EN 15380 Terminology Mapping**:
| Common Term | EN 15380 Code | Interval | Duration |
|-------------|---------------|----------|----------|
| A-Check | PM1 | 15,000 km | 15-60 min |
| B-Check | PM2 | 45,000 km | 4-16 hours |
| C-Check | HM | 90,000 km | 1-3 days |
| D-Overhaul | OVH | 500,000-800,000 km | 2-6 weeks |

**KPI Formulas**:
```javascript
// Km Variance (coefficient of variation) - PRIMARY METRIC
const kmVariance = (standardDeviation(currentKms) / mean(currentKms)) * 100;

// Fleet Availability
const availability = (fitTrains / totalTrains) * 100;

// Depot Utilization
const utilization = (scheduledTracks / availableTracks) * 100;

// Train Delta (individual vs fleet average)
const trainDelta = ((trainKm - fleetAvgKm) / fleetAvgKm) * 100;

// Maintenance Window Efficiency
const windowEfficiency = actualWorkHours / scheduledWindowHours * 100;
```

1Ôćĺ# @belt/tools Analysis
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Package Info
     4Ôćĺ
     5Ôćĺ| Property | Value |
     6Ôćĺ|----------|-------|
     7Ôćĺ| **Name** | `@belt/tools` |
     8Ôćĺ| **Version** | `0.0.1` |
     9Ôćĺ| **Published** | 5 hours ago (as of 2026-01-05) |
    10Ôćĺ| **License** | Proprietary |
    11Ôćĺ| **Repository** | https://github.com/zazcart/tools (404 - private repository) |
    12Ôćĺ| **Maintainer** | antonio.presto <antonio@paper.sh> |
    13Ôćĺ| **Unpacked Size** | 503.9 kB |
    14Ôćĺ| **Description** | TypeScript utility library with runtime type validation, state management, and common helpers |
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ### Dependencies
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ| Package | Version | Purpose |
    19Ôćĺ|---------|---------|---------|
    20Ôćĺ| `date-fns` | ^4.1.0 | Date utilities |
    21Ôćĺ| `immer` | ^11.1.3 | Immutable state updates |
    22Ôćĺ| `lodash` | ^4.17.21 | General utilities |
    23Ôćĺ| `lru-cache` | ^11.2.4 | LRU caching |
    24Ôćĺ| `minimatch` | ^10.1.1 | Glob matching |
    25Ôćĺ| `typebox` | ^1.0.73 | Runtime type validation |
    26Ôćĺ
    27Ôćĺ---
    28Ôćĺ
    29Ôćĺ## Local Toolkit (`@zazcart/tools`)
    30Ôćĺ
    31ÔćĺThe project currently uses a **local package** at `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/system/packages/toolkit/` with the npm name `@zazcart/tools`. The local toolkit and the published `@belt/tools` appear to be **the same library** from the same maintainer, just under different package names.
    32Ôćĺ
    33Ôćĺ### Local Package Dependencies
    34Ôćĺ
    35Ôćĺ| Package | Version |
    36Ôćĺ|---------|---------|
    37Ôćĺ| `date-fns` | 4.1.0 |
    38Ôćĺ| `immer` | 10.2.0 |
    39Ôćĺ| `lodash` | 4.17.21 |
    40Ôćĺ| `lru-cache` | 11.2.2 |
    41Ôćĺ| `minimatch` | 10.1.1 |
    42Ôćĺ| `typebox` | 1.0.50 |
    43Ôćĺ
    44Ôćĺ---
    45Ôćĺ
    46Ôćĺ## Core API
    47Ôćĺ
    48Ôćĺ### createSlice / createSyncSlice
    49Ôćĺ
    50ÔćĺFactory function to create a synchronous state slice with Immer-powered reducers.
    51Ôćĺ
    52Ôćĺ```typescript
    53Ôćĺimport { createSlice, createSyncSlice } from '@zazcart/tools';
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺconst counterSlice = createSlice({
    56Ôćĺ  id: 'counter',
    57Ôćĺ  initial: { value: 0 },
    58Ôćĺ  cache: { policy: 'private' },
    59Ôćĺ  reducers: {
    60Ôćĺ    increment(state) {
    61Ôćĺ      state.value += 1;  // Immer handles immutability
    62Ôćĺ    },
    63Ôćĺ    add(state, payload: number) {
    64Ôćĺ      state.value += payload;
    65Ôćĺ    },
    66Ôćĺ    reset(state) {
    67Ôćĺ      state.value = 0;
    68Ôćĺ    },
    69Ôćĺ  },
    70Ôćĺ});
    71Ôćĺ```
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ#### Parameters
    74Ôćĺ
    75Ôćĺ| Field | Type | Required | Description |
    76Ôćĺ|-------|------|----------|-------------|
    77Ôćĺ| `id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier for the slice |
    78Ôćĺ| `initial` | `State` | Yes | Initial state value |
    79Ôćĺ| `cache` | `CacheStrategy` | Yes | Cache invalidation policy |
    80Ôćĺ| `reducers` | `Record<string, SliceReducer>` | Yes | Object of reducer functions |
    81Ôćĺ
    82Ôćĺ### createAsyncSlice
    83Ôćĺ
    84ÔćĺFactory function for async data fetching slices with built-in loading/error states.
    85Ôćĺ
    86Ôćĺ```typescript
    87Ôćĺimport { createAsyncSlice } from '@zazcart/tools';
    88Ôćĺ
    89Ôćĺconst userSlice = createAsyncSlice({
    90Ôćĺ  id: 'user',
    91Ôćĺ  cache: { policy: 'private' },
    92Ôćĺ  context: {
    93Ôćĺ    api: { loadUser: async (id: string) => fetch(`/users/${id}`) },
    94Ôćĺ  },
    95Ôćĺ  load: async (userId: string, context) => {
    96Ôćĺ    return context.api.loadUser(userId);
    97Ôćĺ  },
    98Ôćĺ});
    99Ôćĺ```
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ#### Async Slice State Shape
   102Ôćĺ
   103Ôćĺ```typescript
   104Ôćĺinterface AsyncSliceState<Value> {
   105Ôćĺ  loading: boolean;      // True during fetch
   106Ôćĺ  loaded: boolean;       // True after successful fetch
   107Ôćĺ  failed: boolean;       // True if fetch failed
   108Ôćĺ  error: string | null;  // Error message
   109Ôćĺ  value: Value | null;   // Fetched data
   110Ôćĺ  changes: number;       // Update counter
   111Ôćĺ}
   112Ôćĺ```
   113Ôćĺ
   114Ôćĺ#### Auto-generated Async Actions
   115Ôćĺ
   116Ôćĺ| Action | Effect |
   117Ôćĺ|--------|--------|
   118Ôćĺ| `load(payload)` | Sets `loading: true`, `failed: false` |
   119Ôćĺ| `success(value)` | Sets `loading: false`, `loaded: true`, stores value |
   120Ôćĺ| `fail({ error })` | Sets `loading: false`, `failed: true`, stores error |
   121Ôćĺ| `clear()` | Resets to initial state |
   122Ôćĺ
   123Ôćĺ### createMachine
   124Ôćĺ
   125ÔćĺCombines multiple slices into a unified state machine with React hooks.
   126Ôćĺ
   127Ôćĺ```typescript
   128Ôćĺimport { createMachine } from '@zazcart/tools';
   129Ôćĺ
   130Ôćĺconst appMachine = createMachine({
   131Ôćĺ  id: 'app',
   132Ôćĺ  slices: {
   133Ôćĺ    user: userSlice,
   134Ôćĺ    counter: counterSlice,
   135Ôćĺ  },
   136Ôćĺ  context: {
   137Ôćĺ    userID: 'user-123',
   138Ôćĺ    api: { loadUser },
   139Ôćĺ  },
   140Ôćĺ  selectors: {
   141Ôćĺ    doubledCounter: state => state.counter.value * 2,
   142Ôćĺ  },
   143Ôćĺ  metadata: true,  // Enable metadata tracking
   144Ôćĺ  sessionID: 'unique-session-id',
   145Ôćĺ});
   146Ôćĺ
   147Ôćĺ// Exports
   148ÔćĺappMachine.useMachine;        // React hook
   149ÔćĺappMachine.useSelector;       // Selector hook
   150ÔćĺappMachine.actions;           // Action creators by slice
   151ÔćĺappMachine.actionTypes;       // Action type strings
   152ÔćĺappMachine.getState();        // Current state
   153ÔćĺappMachine.dispatch(action);  // Dispatch action
   154ÔćĺappMachine.subscribe(fn);     // Subscribe to changes
   155ÔćĺappMachine.reset();           // Reset to initial state
   156Ôćĺ```
   157Ôćĺ
   158Ôćĺ#### Machine Config Options
   159Ôćĺ
   160Ôćĺ| Field | Type | Default | Description |
   161Ôćĺ|-------|------|---------|-------------|
   162Ôćĺ| `id` | `string` | - | Unique machine identifier |
   163Ôćĺ| `slices` | `Record<string, Slice>` | - | State slices |
   164Ôćĺ| `context` | `object` | `{}` | Shared context (API clients, etc.) |
   165Ôćĺ| `initial` | `Partial<State>` | - | Override initial state (hydration) |
   166Ôćĺ| `selectors` | `Record<string, Selector>` | - | Derived state functions |
   167Ôćĺ| `metadata` | `boolean` | `true` | Track changes/timestamps |
   168Ôćĺ| `sessionID` | `string` | `GLOBAL_APP_SESSION_ID` | Session identifier |
   169Ôćĺ
   170Ôćĺ---
   171Ôćĺ
   172Ôćĺ## Cache Policies
   173Ôćĺ
   174ÔćĺThe library implements sophisticated cache invalidation based on three policies:
   175Ôćĺ
   176Ôćĺ### Policy Types
   177Ôćĺ
   178Ôćĺ```typescript
   179Ôćĺenum CachePolicy {
   180Ôćĺ  public = 'public',   // Shared, invalidated only by TTL
   181Ôćĺ  private = 'private', // User-bound, invalidated on user change
   182Ôćĺ  session = 'session', // Session-bound, invalidated on app restart
   183Ôćĺ}
   184Ôćĺ
   185Ôćĺtype CacheStrategy = {
   186Ôćĺ  policy: 'public' | 'private' | 'session';
   187Ôćĺ  ttl?: string;      // Time-to-live: '1h', '30m', '7d'
   188Ôćĺ  swr?: boolean;     // Stale-while-revalidate
   189Ôćĺ};
   190Ôćĺ```
   191Ôćĺ
   192Ôćĺ### Policy Descriptions
   193Ôćĺ
   194Ôćĺ| Policy | Invalidated When | Use Case |
   195Ôćĺ|--------|------------------|----------|
   196Ôćĺ| `public` | TTL expires only | Feature flags, pricing, static config |
   197Ôćĺ| `private` | User changes OR TTL expires | User profile, permissions, preferences |
   198Ôćĺ| `session` | App restarts OR TTL expires | Temporary UI state, form drafts |
   199Ôćĺ
   200Ôćĺ### TTL Format (MSTimeString)
   201Ôćĺ
   202ÔćĺSupports human-readable duration strings:
   203Ôćĺ
   204Ôćĺ```typescript
   205Ôćĺ'1h'   // 1 hour
   206Ôćĺ'30m'  // 30 minutes
   207Ôćĺ'7d'   // 7 days
   208Ôćĺ'500'  // 500 milliseconds (numeric string)
   209Ôćĺ```
   210Ôćĺ
   211Ôćĺ### Stale-While-Revalidate (SWR)
   212Ôćĺ
   213ÔćĺWhen `swr: true`:
   214Ôćĺ- Returns cached data immediately (even if stale)
   215Ôćĺ- Triggers background revalidation
   216Ôćĺ- Updates UI when fresh data arrives
   217Ôćĺ
   218Ôćĺ---
   219Ôćĺ
   220Ôćĺ## Performance Options
   221Ôćĺ
   222Ôćĺ### `metadata: false` (trackMetadata)
   223Ôćĺ
   224Ôćĺ**Critical for 60fps applications.**
   225Ôćĺ
   226Ôćĺ```typescript
   227Ôćĺconst canvasMachine = createMachine({
   228Ôćĺ  id: 'canvas',
   229Ôćĺ  slices: { viewport: viewportSlice },
   230Ôćĺ  metadata: false,  // <-- Disable for high-frequency updates
   231Ôćĺ});
   232Ôćĺ```
   233Ôćĺ
   234Ôćĺ#### What Metadata Tracks
   235Ôćĺ
   236ÔćĺWhen `metadata: true` (default):
   237Ôćĺ- `changes`: Counter incremented on each dispatch
   238Ôćĺ- `updatedAt`: Timestamp of last update
   239Ôćĺ- `updatedByActionType`: Action that caused the update
   240Ôćĺ- `userID`, `sessionID`: For cache invalidation
   241Ôćĺ
   242Ôćĺ#### Why Disable for Animation Loops
   243Ôćĺ
   244Ôćĺ```typescript
   245Ôćĺ// With metadata: true (default)
   246Ôćĺ// Every dispatch creates new metadata object:
   247Ôćĺ{
   248Ôćĺ  ...state,
   249Ôćĺ  '~metadata': {
   250Ôćĺ    changes: previousChanges + 1,  // New object allocation
   251Ôćĺ    updatedAt: Date.now(),
   252Ôćĺ    updatedByActionType: action.type,
   253Ôćĺ    // ...
   254Ôćĺ  }
   255Ôćĺ}
   256Ôćĺ// This triggers GC on every frame at 60fps = GC thrashing
   257Ôćĺ```
   258Ôćĺ
   259Ôćĺ#### When to Disable
   260Ôćĺ
   261Ôćĺ| Use Case | `metadata` Value |
   262Ôćĺ|----------|------------------|
   263Ôćĺ| UI state (buttons, modals) | `true` (default) |
   264Ôćĺ| Form state | `true` |
   265Ôćĺ| Canvas/animation state | `false` |
   266Ôćĺ| Game loop state | `false` |
   267Ôćĺ| Real-time streaming | `false` |
   268Ôćĺ
   269Ôćĺ---
   270Ôćĺ
   271Ôćĺ## React Integration: useSyncExternalStore
   272Ôćĺ
   273ÔćĺThe library uses React 18's `useSyncExternalStore` for tear-free reads:
   274Ôćĺ
   275Ôćĺ```typescript
   276Ôćĺ// From hooks.ts
   277Ôćĺfunction useSelector<T>(selector: (state: any) => T): T {
   278Ôćĺ  return React.useSyncExternalStore(store.subscribe, () => {
   279Ôćĺ    const fullState = store.getState();
   280Ôćĺ    return selector(fullState);
   281Ôćĺ  });
   282Ôćĺ}
   283Ôćĺ```
   284Ôćĺ
   285Ôćĺ### Benefits Over useState
   286Ôćĺ
   287Ôćĺ| Problem | useState | @zazcart/tools |
   288Ôćĺ|---------|----------|----------------|
   289Ôćĺ| Stale closures | Common in animation loops | Solved via getState() |
   290Ôćĺ| Batched updates | Async (unreliable timing) | Synchronous |
   291Ôćĺ| Re-render granularity | Coarse | Fine (selectors) |
   292Ôćĺ
   293Ôćĺ---
   294Ôćĺ
   295Ôćĺ## Migration from Local Toolkit
   296Ôćĺ
   297Ôćĺ### Current State
   298Ôćĺ
   299ÔćĺThe MMS project already uses `@zazcart/tools` as a **local package**:
   300Ôćĺ
   301Ôćĺ```json
   302Ôćĺ// packages/toolkit/package.json
   303Ôćĺ{
   304Ôćĺ  "name": "@zazcart/tools",
   305Ôćĺ  "version": "0.0.1"
   306Ôćĺ}
   307Ôćĺ```
   308Ôćĺ
   309Ôćĺ### Migration to npm @belt/tools
   310Ôćĺ
   311ÔćĺSince `@belt/tools` appears to be the published version of the same library:
   312Ôćĺ
   313Ôćĺ#### Step 1: Update package.json
   314Ôćĺ
   315Ôćĺ```bash
   316Ôćĺ# In apps/canvas
   317Ôćĺbun remove @zazcart/tools
   318Ôćĺbun add @belt/tools
   319Ôćĺ```
   320Ôćĺ
   321Ôćĺ#### Step 2: Update imports
   322Ôćĺ
   323Ôćĺ```typescript
   324Ôćĺ// Before
   325Ôćĺimport { createMachine, createSlice } from '@zazcart/tools';
   326Ôćĺ
   327Ôćĺ// After
   328Ôćĺimport { createMachine, createSlice } from '@belt/tools';
   329Ôćĺ```
   330Ôćĺ
   331Ôćĺ#### Step 3: Verify API compatibility
   332Ôćĺ
   333ÔćĺBased on source analysis, the APIs are identical:
   334Ôćĺ
   335Ôćĺ| Export | Local `@zazcart/tools` | npm `@belt/tools` |
   336Ôćĺ|--------|------------------------|-------------------|
   337Ôćĺ| `createMachine` | Yes | Yes (assumed) |
   338Ôćĺ| `createSlice` | Yes | Yes (assumed) |
   339Ôćĺ| `createSyncSlice` | Yes | Yes (assumed) |
   340Ôćĺ| `createAsyncSlice` | Yes | Yes (assumed) |
   341Ôćĺ
   342Ôćĺ---
   343Ôćĺ
   344Ôćĺ## Code Examples
   345Ôćĺ
   346Ôćĺ### Basic Usage (Current MMS Implementation)
   347Ôćĺ
   348Ôćĺ```typescript
   349Ôćĺ// apps/canvas/src/state/viewport.ts
   350Ôćĺimport { createSyncSlice } from '@zazcart/tools';
   351Ôćĺ
   352Ôćĺexport const viewportSlice = createSyncSlice({
   353Ôćĺ  id: 'viewport',
   354Ôćĺ  initial: {
   355Ôćĺ    camera: { x: 0, y: 0 },
   356Ôćĺ    canvasSize: { width: 800, height: 600 },
   357Ôćĺ    isPanning: false,
   358Ôćĺ  },
   359Ôćĺ  cache: { policy: 'session' },
   360Ôćĺ  reducers: {
   361Ôćĺ    setCamera(state, payload: { x: number; y: number }) {
   362Ôćĺ      state.camera = payload;
   363Ôćĺ    },
   364Ôćĺ    startPan(state, payload: { panStart; cameraPosAtPanStart }) {
   365Ôćĺ      state.isPanning = true;
   366Ôćĺ      state.panStart = payload.panStart;
   367Ôćĺ      state.cameraPosAtPanStart = payload.cameraPosAtPanStart;
   368Ôćĺ    },
   369Ôćĺ    endPan(state) {
   370Ôćĺ      state.isPanning = false;
   371Ôćĺ    },
   372Ôćĺ  },
   373Ôćĺ});
   374Ôćĺ```
   375Ôćĺ
   376Ôćĺ### Machine with Multiple Slices
   377Ôćĺ
   378Ôćĺ```typescript
   379Ôćĺ// apps/canvas/src/state/canvas-machine.ts
   380Ôćĺimport { createMachine } from '@zazcart/tools';
   381Ôćĺimport { viewportSlice } from './viewport';
   382Ôćĺimport { interactionSlice } from './interaction';
   383Ôćĺ
   384Ôćĺexport const canvasMachine = createMachine({
   385Ôćĺ  id: 'canvas',
   386Ôćĺ  slices: {
   387Ôćĺ    viewport: viewportSlice,
   388Ôćĺ    interaction: interactionSlice,
   389Ôćĺ  },
   390Ôćĺ  context: { userID: null },
   391Ôćĺ  metadata: false,  // High-frequency updates
   392Ôćĺ});
   393Ôćĺ
   394Ôćĺ// Typed exports
   395Ôćĺexport const useCanvas = canvasMachine.useMachine;
   396Ôćĺexport const useCanvasSelector = canvasMachine.useSelector;
   397Ôćĺexport const canvasActions = canvasMachine.actions;
   398Ôćĺexport const canvasStore = {
   399Ôćĺ  getState: canvasMachine.getState,
   400Ôćĺ  dispatch: canvasMachine.dispatch,
   401Ôćĺ  subscribe: canvasMachine.subscribe,
   402Ôćĺ};
   403Ôćĺ```
   404Ôćĺ
   405Ôćĺ### Usage in React Components
   406Ôćĺ
   407Ôćĺ```typescript
   408Ôćĺ// Reading state
   409Ôćĺconst camera = useCanvasSelector(s => s.viewport.camera);
   410Ôćĺ
   411Ôćĺ// Dispatching actions
   412Ôćĺimport { canvasStore, canvasActions } from '../state';
   413ÔćĺcanvasStore.dispatch(canvasActions.viewport.setCamera({ x: 100, y: 200 }));
   414Ôćĺ
   415Ôćĺ// In animation loops (synchronous read)
   416Ôćĺfunction animate() {
   417Ôćĺ  const state = canvasStore.getState();
   418Ôćĺ  const { x, y } = state.viewport.camera;
   419Ôćĺ  // Use values synchronously
   420Ôćĺ  requestAnimationFrame(animate);
   421Ôćĺ}
   422Ôćĺ```
   423Ôćĺ
   424Ôćĺ### Async Slice Example
   425Ôćĺ
   426Ôćĺ```typescript
   427Ôćĺconst userSlice = createAsyncSlice({
   428Ôćĺ  id: 'user',
   429Ôćĺ  cache: { policy: 'private', ttl: '30m', swr: true },
   430Ôćĺ  context: {
   431Ôćĺ    api: { loadUser: async (id: string) => fetch(`/api/users/${id}`).then(r => r.json()) },
   432Ôćĺ  },
   433Ôćĺ  load: async (userId: string, context) => {
   434Ôćĺ    return context.api.loadUser(userId);
   435Ôćĺ  },
   436Ôćĺ});
   437Ôćĺ
   438Ôćĺ// Usage
   439Ôćĺmachine.dispatch(machine.actions.user.load('user-123'));
   440Ôćĺ// ... later after async completion
   441Ôćĺmachine.dispatch(machine.actions.user.success({ id: 'user-123', name: 'John' }));
   442Ôćĺ```
   443Ôćĺ
   444Ôćĺ---
   445Ôćĺ
   446Ôćĺ## Comparison Table
   447Ôćĺ
   448Ôćĺ| Feature | Local `@zazcart/tools` | npm `@belt/tools` |
   449Ôćĺ|---------|------------------------|-------------------|
   450Ôćĺ| **Package Name** | `@zazcart/tools` | `@belt/tools` |
   451Ôćĺ| **Version** | 0.0.1 | 0.0.1 |
   452Ôćĺ| **Source** | Local package | npm registry |
   453Ôćĺ| **Repository** | Local files | Private GitHub |
   454Ôćĺ| **Immer Version** | 10.2.0 | ^11.1.3 (newer) |
   455Ôćĺ| **date-fns Version** | 4.1.0 | ^4.1.0 |
   456Ôćĺ| **LRU Cache** | 11.2.2 | ^11.2.4 (newer) |
   457Ôćĺ| **createMachine** | Yes | Yes |
   458Ôćĺ| **createSlice** | Yes | Yes |
   459Ôćĺ| **createSyncSlice** | Yes | Yes |
   460Ôćĺ| **createAsyncSlice** | Yes | Yes |
   461Ôćĺ| **Cache Policies** | public/private/session | public/private/session |
   462Ôćĺ| **metadata option** | Yes | Yes |
   463Ôćĺ| **useSyncExternalStore** | Yes | Yes |
   464Ôćĺ
   465Ôćĺ### Key Differences
   466Ôćĺ
   467Ôćĺ1. **Immer version**: npm version uses ^11.1.3, local uses 10.2.0
   468Ôćĺ   - May have minor performance improvements
   469Ôćĺ   - API is compatible
   470Ôćĺ
   471Ôćĺ2. **Source control**: npm version is controlled via npm releases vs local git
   472Ôćĺ
   473Ôćĺ3. **Bundle**: npm version is pre-built (503.9 kB), local builds via Vite
   474Ôćĺ
   475Ôćĺ---
   476Ôćĺ
   477Ôćĺ## Recommendations
   478Ôćĺ
   479Ôćĺ### For This Project
   480Ôćĺ
   481Ôćĺ1. **No immediate migration needed**: The local `@zazcart/tools` and npm `@belt/tools` are effectively the same library
   482Ôćĺ
   483Ôćĺ2. **Consider npm migration when**:
   484Ôćĺ   - Multiple projects need to share the library
   485Ôćĺ   - Versioning and changelogs become important
   486Ôćĺ   - CI/CD benefits from npm caching
   487Ôćĺ
   488Ôćĺ3. **Keep `metadata: false`**: The canvas already correctly uses this for 60fps performance
   489Ôćĺ
   490Ôćĺ4. **Cache policies are well-used**: `session` policy for viewport/interaction is correct
   491Ôćĺ
   492Ôćĺ### Performance Best Practices (from source analysis)
   493Ôćĺ
   494Ôćĺ1. **Use `metadata: false`** for animation state machines
   495Ôćĺ2. **Use getState() in animation loops** instead of React hooks
   496Ôćĺ3. **Use fine-grained selectors** to minimize re-renders
   497Ôćĺ4. **Dispatch actions directly** instead of through React state
   498Ôćĺ
   499Ôćĺ---
   500Ôćĺ
   501Ôćĺ## Known Gaps
   502Ôćĺ
   503ÔćĺSince the GitHub repository returns 404 (private):
   504Ôćĺ
   505Ôćĺ- No public changelog available
   506Ôćĺ- No public issue tracker
   507Ôćĺ- Type definitions must be inferred from usage
   508Ôćĺ- No documentation beyond source code
   509Ôćĺ
   510ÔćĺThe published npm package may have additional exports or features not present in the local version. Verify by inspecting the npm tarball directly if needed:
   511Ôćĺ
   512Ôćĺ```bash
   513Ôćĺnpm pack @belt/tools --dry-run
   514Ôćĺ```

1Ôćĺ# PatternFly v6 Research for MMS Migration 2Ôćĺ 3Ôćĺ> Research Date: 2026-01-05 4Ôćĺ> Sources: [PatternFly Official Documentation](https://www.patternfly.org/), [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react) 5Ôćĺ 6Ôćĺ--- 7Ôćĺ 8Ôćĺ## Package Overview 9Ôćĺ 10ÔćĺPatternFly is Red Hat's open source design system for enterprise applications. Version 6.4.0 is current (October 2025). 11Ôćĺ 12Ôćĺ### Core Packages 13Ôćĺ 14Ôćĺ| Package | Purpose | Version | 15Ôćĺ|---------|---------|---------| 16Ôćĺ| `@patternfly/react-core` | Primary component library | 6.4.0 | 17Ôćĺ| `@patternfly/react-table` | Table components | 6.x | 18Ôćĺ| `@patternfly/react-charts` | Victory-based charting | 8.1.0 | 19Ôćĺ| `@patternfly/react-topology` | Graph/network visualization | 6.3.0 | 20Ôćĺ| `@patternfly/react-icons` | Icon components | 6.x | 21Ôćĺ| `@patternfly/react-tokens` | CSS design tokens | 6.x | 22Ôćĺ| `@patternfly/react-styles` | CSS styling utilities | 6.x | 23Ôćĺ 24Ôćĺ### Installation 25Ôćĺ 26Ôćĺ```bash 27Ôćĺ# Core installation 28Ôćĺbun add @patternfly/react-core @patternfly/react-icons 29Ôćĺ 30Ôćĺ# For charts (optional) 31Ôćĺbun add @patternfly/react-charts victory 32Ôćĺ 33Ôćĺ# For topology (separate repo) 34Ôćĺbun add @patternfly/react-topology 35Ôćĺ 36Ôćĺ# For tables (if needed beyond react-core) 37Ôćĺbun add @patternfly/react-table 38Ôćĺ``` 39Ôćĺ 40Ôćĺ### Required CSS Import 41Ôćĺ 42Ôćĺ```typescript 43Ôćĺ// Place BEFORE main app component import 44Ôćĺimport '@patternfly/react-core/dist/styles/base.css'; 45Ôćĺ 46Ôćĺ// For dark mode charts support 47Ôćĺimport '@patternfly/patternfly/patternfly-charts.css'; 48Ôćĺ``` 49Ôćĺ 50Ôćĺ--- 51Ôćĺ 52Ôćĺ## React Version Compatibility 53Ôćĺ 54Ôćĺ**PatternFly 6 supports React 17, 18, AND 19** (Q2 2025 update). 55Ôćĺ 56ÔćĺThis is ideal for MMS because: 57Ôćĺ- Current stack uses React 18 (required by BlueprintJS 6) 58Ôćĺ- Migration can happen without React version change 59Ôćĺ- Future upgrade path to React 19 is available 60Ôćĺ 61Ôćĺ--- 62Ôćĺ 63Ôćĺ## Core Components for MMS 64Ôćĺ 65Ôćĺ### 1. Page Layout System 66Ôćĺ 67ÔćĺThe `Page` component provides the main application structure: 68Ôćĺ 69Ôćĺ```tsx 70Ôćĺimport { 71Ôćĺ Page, 72Ôćĺ PageSection, 73Ôćĺ PageSidebar, 74Ôćĺ PageSidebarBody, 75Ôćĺ Masthead, 76Ôćĺ MastheadMain, 77Ôćĺ MastheadBrand, 78Ôćĺ MastheadLogo, 79Ôćĺ MastheadContent, 80Ôćĺ MastheadToggle, 81Ôćĺ PageToggleButton 82Ôćĺ} from '@patternfly/react-core'; 83Ôćĺ 84Ôćĺfunction App() { 85Ôćĺ return ( 86Ôćĺ } 88Ôćĺ sidebar={} 89Ôćĺ isManagedSidebar // Auto manages open/close state 90Ôćĺ > 91Ôćĺ 92Ôćĺ {/* Main canvas goes here */} 93Ôćĺ 94Ôćĺ 95Ôćĺ ); 96Ôćĺ} 97Ôćĺ``` 98Ôćĺ 99Ôćĺ**Key Props:** 100Ôćĺ- `isManagedSidebar` - Automatic sidebar state management 101Ôćĺ- `isContentFilled` - Sections fill available vertical space 102Ôćĺ- `breadcrumb`, `horizontalSubnav` - Optional navigation elements 103Ôćĺ 104Ôćĺ### 2. Card Component 105Ôćĺ 106ÔćĺDirect replacement for BlueprintJS Card: 107Ôćĺ 108Ôćĺ```tsx 109Ôćĺimport { 110Ôćĺ Card, 111Ôćĺ CardHeader, 112Ôćĺ CardTitle, 113Ôćĺ CardBody, 114Ôćĺ CardFooter, 115Ôćĺ CardExpandableContent 116Ôćĺ} from '@patternfly/react-core'; 117Ôćĺ 118Ôćĺ// Basic card 119Ôćĺ 120Ôćĺ Train TRAIN-001 121Ôćĺ Maintenance scheduled 122Ôćĺ 123Ôćĺ 124Ôćĺ// Selectable card (for task selection) 125Ôćĺ 131Ôćĺ Task content 132Ôćĺ 133Ôćĺ 134Ôćĺ// Expandable card 135Ôćĺ 136Ôćĺ 137Ôćĺ Details 138Ôćĺ 139Ôćĺ 140Ôćĺ Expanded content 141Ôćĺ 142Ôćĺ 143Ôćĺ``` 144Ôćĺ 145Ôćĺ**Variants:** 146Ôćĺ- `isCompact` - Smaller padding 147Ôćĺ- `isLarge` - Larger sizing 148Ôćĺ- `isPlain` - No border/background 149Ôćĺ- `variant="secondary"` - Secondary background color 150Ôćĺ 151Ôćĺ### 3. Label Component (Tag Replacement) 152Ôćĺ 153ÔćĺReplaces BlueprintJS Tag: 154Ôćĺ 155Ôćĺ```tsx 156Ôćĺimport { Label, LabelGroup } from '@patternfly/react-core'; 157Ôćĺ 158Ôćĺ// Basic labels with colors 159Ôćĺ 160Ôćĺ 161Ôćĺ 162Ôćĺ 163Ôćĺ// Status labels (for LOD indicator) 164Ôćĺ 165Ôćĺ 166Ôćĺ 167Ôćĺ// With icon 168Ôćĺ 169Ôćĺ 170Ôćĺ// Removable label 171Ôćĺ 172Ôćĺ 173Ôćĺ// Label group 174Ôćĺ 175Ôćĺ 176Ôćĺ 177Ôćĺ 178Ôćĺ``` 179Ôćĺ 180Ôćĺ**Colors Available:** 181Ôćĺ- Non-status: blue, teal, green, orange, purple, red, orange-red, grey, yellow 182Ôćĺ- Status: success (green), warning (yellow), danger (red), info (purple) 183Ôćĺ 184Ôćĺ**Variants:** 185Ôćĺ- `filled` - Solid background (default) 186Ôćĺ- `outline` - Border only 187Ôćĺ- `compact` - Reduced padding 188Ôćĺ 189Ôćĺ### 4. Button Component 190Ôćĺ 191Ôćĺ```tsx 192Ôćĺimport { Button } from '@patternfly/react-core'; 193Ôćĺ 194Ôćĺ// Variants 195Ôćĺ 196Ôćĺ 197Ôćĺ 198Ôćĺ 199Ôćĺ 200Ôćĺ 201Ôćĺ 205Ôćĺ 206Ôćĺ// Sizes 207Ôćĺ 208Ôćĺ 209Ôćĺ 210Ôćĺ// With loading state 211Ôćĺ 212Ôćĺ 213Ôćĺ// Disabled states 214Ôćĺ 215Ôćĺ // Allows tooltip 216Ôćĺ``` 217Ôćĺ 218Ôćĺ### 5. Toolbar (for controls area) 219Ôćĺ 220ÔćĺReplaces ButtonGroup and control containers: 221Ôćĺ 222Ôćĺ```tsx 223Ôćĺimport { 224Ôćĺ Toolbar, 225Ôćĺ ToolbarContent, 226Ôćĺ ToolbarGroup, 227Ôćĺ ToolbarItem 228Ôćĺ} from '@patternfly/react-core'; 229Ôćĺ 230Ôćĺ 231Ôćĺ 232Ôćĺ {/* Action buttons group */} 233Ôćĺ 234Ôćĺ 235Ôćĺ 236Ôćĺ 237Ôćĺ 238Ôćĺ 239Ôćĺ 240Ôćĺ 241Ôćĺ 242Ôćĺ {/* Label item */} 243Ôćĺ 244Ôćĺ 245Ôćĺ 246Ôćĺ 247Ôćĺ {/* Separator */} 248Ôćĺ 249Ôćĺ 250Ôćĺ {/* Pagination (right aligned) */} 251Ôćĺ 252Ôćĺ 253Ôćĺ 254Ôćĺ 255Ôćĺ 256Ôćĺ``` 257Ôćĺ 258Ôćĺ### 6. Navigation (Sidebar) 259Ôćĺ 260Ôćĺ```tsx 261Ôćĺimport { 262Ôćĺ Nav, 263Ôćĺ NavList, 264Ôćĺ NavItem, 265Ôćĺ NavExpandable 266Ôćĺ} from '@patternfly/react-core'; 267Ôćĺ 268Ôćĺ
269Ôćĺ 270Ôćĺ Dashboard 271Ôćĺ Planning 272Ôćĺ 273Ôćĺ TRAIN-001 274Ôćĺ TRAIN-002 275Ôćĺ 276Ôćĺ 277Ôćĺ
278Ôćĺ``` 279Ôćĺ 280Ôćĺ### 7. Drawer (Side Panel for Details) 281Ôćĺ 282Ôćĺ```tsx 283Ôćĺimport { 284Ôćĺ Drawer, 285Ôćĺ DrawerContent, 286Ôćĺ DrawerPanelContent, 287Ôćĺ DrawerHead, 288Ôćĺ DrawerActions, 289Ôćĺ DrawerCloseButton, 290Ôćĺ DrawerPanelBody 291Ôćĺ} from '@patternfly/react-core'; 292Ôćĺ 293Ôćĺ 294Ôćĺ 296Ôćĺ 297Ôćĺ Task Details 298Ôćĺ 299Ôćĺ 300Ôćĺ 301Ôćĺ 302Ôćĺ 303Ôćĺ {/* Task detail content */} 304Ôćĺ 305Ôćĺ 306Ôćĺ }> 307Ôćĺ {/* Main content (canvas) */} 308Ôćĺ 309Ôćĺ 310Ôćĺ 311Ôćĺ``` 312Ôćĺ 313Ôćĺ**Position Options:** 314Ôćĺ- `end` (default) - Right side 315Ôćĺ- `start` - Left side 316Ôćĺ- `bottom` - Bottom panel 317Ôćĺ 318Ôćĺ--- 319Ôćĺ 320Ôćĺ## Layout System 321Ôćĺ 322Ôćĺ### Grid Layout 323Ôćĺ 324Ôćĺ```tsx 325Ôćĺimport { Grid, GridItem } from '@patternfly/react-core'; 326Ôćĺ 327Ôćĺ 328Ôćĺ Sidebar 329Ôćĺ Main Content 330Ôćĺ 331Ôćĺ 332Ôćĺ// Responsive 333Ôćĺ 338Ôćĺ Content 339Ôćĺ 340Ôćĺ``` 341Ôćĺ 342Ôćĺ### PageSection Types 343Ôćĺ 344Ôćĺ```tsx 345ÔćĺMain content 346ÔćĺSub navigation 347ÔćĺBreadcrumbs 348ÔćĺTab navigation 349ÔćĺWizard content 350Ôćĺ``` 351Ôćĺ 352Ôćĺ--- 353Ôćĺ 354Ôćĺ## WebGL Integration Strategy 355Ôćĺ 356ÔćĺPatternFly components are standard React components - no special handling needed for WebGL/Three.js: 357Ôćĺ 358Ôćĺ### Approach 1: PageSection Container 359Ôćĺ 360Ôćĺ```tsx 361Ôćĺimport { Page, PageSection } from '@patternfly/react-core'; 362Ôćĺimport { Canvas } from '@react-three/fiber'; 363Ôćĺ 364Ôćĺfunction App() { 365Ôćĺ return ( 366Ôćĺ 367Ôćĺ 372Ôćĺ 376Ôćĺ 377Ôćĺ 378Ôćĺ 379Ôćĺ 380Ôćĺ ); 381Ôćĺ} 382Ôćĺ``` 383Ôćĺ 384Ôćĺ### Approach 2: Card Container (for embedded canvas) 385Ôćĺ 386Ôćĺ```tsx 387Ôćĺ 388Ôćĺ 389Ôćĺ 390Ôćĺ {/* Three.js content */} 391Ôćĺ 392Ôćĺ 393Ôćĺ 394Ôćĺ``` 395Ôćĺ 396Ôćĺ### Approach 3: Drawer with Canvas 397Ôćĺ 398Ôćĺ```tsx 399Ôćĺ 400Ôćĺ }> 401Ôćĺ
402Ôćĺ 403Ôćĺ 404Ôćĺ 405Ôćĺ
406Ôćĺ 407Ôćĺ 408Ôćĺ``` 409Ôćĺ 410Ôćĺ**Key Considerations:** 411Ôćĺ1. Use `padding={{ default: 'noPadding' }}` on PageSection for full-bleed canvas 412Ôćĺ2. Set explicit heights or use flexbox/grid for proper canvas sizing 413Ôćĺ3. Canvas needs `position: absolute/relative` within container 414Ôćĺ4. Use `isFilled` prop for sections that should expand 415Ôćĺ 416Ôćĺ--- 417Ôćĺ 418Ôćĺ## BlueprintJS to PatternFly Migration Map 419Ôćĺ 420Ôćĺ| BlueprintJS | PatternFly | Notes | 421Ôćĺ|-------------|------------|-------| 422Ôćĺ| `Card` | `Card` | Nearly identical API | 423Ôćĺ| `Tag` | `Label` | Same concept, different name | 424Ôćĺ| `Button` | `Button` | Similar variants | 425Ôćĺ| `ButtonGroup` | `ToolbarGroup` | Use with Toolbar | 426Ôćĺ| `Navbar` | `Masthead` | Different structure | 427Ôćĺ| `Intent.PRIMARY` | `variant="primary"` | Color as variant | 428Ôćĺ| `Intent.DANGER` | `variant="danger"` or `isDanger` | | 429Ôćĺ| `Intent.WARNING` | `variant="warning"` | | 430Ôćĺ| `Intent.SUCCESS` | `color="green"` (Label) | | 431Ôćĺ| `Collapse` | `CardExpandableContent` | Or `Expandable` | 432Ôćĺ| `Drawer` | `Drawer` | Similar concept | 433Ôćĺ| `Tabs` | `Tabs` | Available in react-core | 434Ôćĺ| `Spinner` | `Spinner` | Available | 435Ôćĺ| `ProgressBar` | `Progress` | Available | 436Ôćĺ 437Ôćĺ### Intent Colors Mapping 438Ôćĺ 439Ôćĺ```tsx 440Ôćĺ// BlueprintJS 441ÔćĺBlue 442ÔćĺRed 443ÔćĺOrange 444ÔćĺGreen 445Ôćĺ 446Ôćĺ// PatternFly equivalent 447Ôćĺ 448Ôćĺ // or status="danger" 449Ôćĺ // or status="warning" 450Ôćĺ // or status="success" 451Ôćĺ``` 452Ôćĺ 453Ôćĺ### Button Migration 454Ôćĺ 455Ôćĺ```tsx 456Ôćĺ// BlueprintJS 457Ôćĺ 458Ôćĺ 459Ôćĺ 460Ôćĺ 461Ôćĺ 462Ôćĺ// PatternFly 463Ôćĺ 464Ôćĺ 465Ôćĺ 466Ôćĺ 467Ôćĺ``` 468Ôćĺ 469Ôćĺ--- 470Ôćĺ 471Ôćĺ## Charts Integration 472Ôćĺ 473ÔćĺPatternFly uses Victory library for charts: 474Ôćĺ 475Ôćĺ```tsx 476Ôćĺimport { Chart, ChartArea, ChartAxis, ChartLine } from '@patternfly/react-charts/victory'; 477Ôćĺ 478Ôćĺ// Basic line chart 479Ôćĺ 483Ôćĺ 484Ôćĺ 485Ôćĺ 490Ôćĺ 491Ôćĺ``` 492Ôćĺ 493Ôćĺ**Note:** Victory is optional peer dependency in v6. Import from `@patternfly/react-charts/victory`. 494Ôćĺ 495Ôćĺ--- 496Ôćĺ 497Ôćĺ## Topology Component 498Ôćĺ 499ÔćĺFor network/dependency visualization (future MMS feature): 500Ôćĺ 501Ôćĺ```tsx 502Ôćĺimport { 503Ôćĺ Visualization, 504Ôćĺ VisualizationProvider, 505Ôćĺ VisualizationSurface, 506Ôćĺ Model, 507Ôćĺ GRAPH_LAYOUT_FORCE 508Ôćĺ} from '@patternfly/react-topology'; 509Ôćĺ 510Ôćĺconst model: Model = { 511Ôćĺ nodes: [...], 512Ôćĺ edges: [...], 513Ôćĺ graph: { 514Ôćĺ id: 'graph', 515Ôćĺ type: 'graph', 516Ôćĺ layout: GRAPH_LAYOUT_FORCE 517Ôćĺ } 518Ôćĺ}; 519Ôćĺ 520Ôćĺ 521Ôćĺ 522Ôćĺ 523Ôćĺ``` 524Ôćĺ 525Ôćĺ--- 526Ôćĺ 527Ôćĺ## Code Examples 528Ôćĺ 529Ôćĺ### Complete App Shell 530Ôćĺ 531Ôćĺ```tsx 532Ôćĺimport React, { useState } from 'react'; 533Ôćĺimport '@patternfly/react-core/dist/styles/base.css'; 534Ôćĺ 535Ôćĺimport { 536Ôćĺ Page, 537Ôćĺ PageSection, 538Ôćĺ PageSidebar, 539Ôćĺ PageSidebarBody, 540Ôćĺ Masthead, 541Ôćĺ MastheadMain, 542Ôćĺ MastheadBrand, 543Ôćĺ MastheadLogo, 544Ôćĺ MastheadContent, 545Ôćĺ MastheadToggle, 546Ôćĺ PageToggleButton, 547Ôćĺ Toolbar, 548Ôćĺ ToolbarContent, 549Ôćĺ ToolbarGroup, 550Ôćĺ ToolbarItem, 551Ôćĺ Button, 552Ôćĺ Label, 553Ôćĺ Nav, 554Ôćĺ NavList, 555Ôćĺ NavItem, 556Ôćĺ Drawer, 557Ôćĺ DrawerContent, 558Ôćĺ DrawerPanelContent, 559Ôćĺ DrawerHead, 560Ôćĺ DrawerActions, 561Ôćĺ DrawerCloseButton, 562Ôćĺ DrawerPanelBody 563Ôćĺ} from '@patternfly/react-core'; 564Ôćĺimport { BarsIcon } from '@patternfly/react-icons'; 565Ôćĺimport { Canvas } from '@react-three/fiber'; 566Ôćĺ 567Ôćĺfunction App() { 568Ôćĺ const [sidebarOpen, setSidebarOpen] = useState(true); 569Ôćĺ const [detailsOpen, setDetailsOpen] = useState(false); 570Ôćĺ const [lod, setLod] = useState<'MACRO' | 'MESO' | 'MICRO'>('MESO'); 571Ôćĺ 572Ôćĺ const masthead = ( 573Ôćĺ 574Ôćĺ 575Ôćĺ 576Ôćĺ setSidebarOpen(!sidebarOpen)} 581Ôćĺ > 582Ôćĺ 583Ôćĺ 584Ôćĺ 585Ôćĺ 586Ôćĺ MMS Planning 587Ôćĺ 588Ôćĺ 589Ôćĺ 590Ôćĺ 591Ôćĺ 592Ôćĺ 593Ôćĺ 594Ôćĺ 595Ôćĺ 596Ôćĺ 597Ôćĺ 598Ôćĺ 599Ôćĺ 600Ôćĺ 601Ôćĺ 604Ôćĺ 605Ôćĺ 606Ôćĺ 607Ôćĺ 608Ôćĺ 609Ôćĺ 610Ôćĺ 611Ôćĺ 612Ôćĺ ); 624Ôćĺ 625Ôćĺ 626Ôćĺ ); 627Ôćĺ 628Ôćĺ const detailsPanel = ( 629Ôćĺ 630Ôćĺ 631Ôćĺ Task Details 632Ôćĺ 633Ôćĺ setDetailsOpen(false)} /> 634Ôćĺ 635Ôćĺ 636Ôćĺ 637Ôćĺ {/* Task details content */} 638Ôćĺ 639Ôćĺ 640Ôćĺ ); 641Ôćĺ 642Ôćĺ return ( 643Ôćĺ 644Ôćĺ 645Ôćĺ 646Ôćĺ 647Ôćĺ

652Ôćĺ 653Ôćĺ 654Ôćĺ 655Ôćĺ 656Ôćĺ ); 657Ôćĺ} 658Ôćĺ 659Ôćĺexport default App; 660Ôćĺ``` 661Ôćĺ 662Ôćĺ### Train Sidebar Component 663Ôćĺ 664Ôćĺ```tsx 665Ôćĺimport { Card, CardBody, Label } from '@patternfly/react-core'; 666Ôćĺ 667Ôćĺinterface TrainSidebarProps { 668Ôćĺ trains: Train[]; 669Ôćĺ visibleRange: [number, number]; 670Ôćĺ} 671Ôćĺ 672Ôćĺexport function TrainSidebar({ trains, visibleRange }: TrainSidebarProps) { 673Ôćĺ const [start, end] = visibleRange; 674Ôćĺ const visibleTrains = trains.slice(start, end); 675Ôćĺ 676Ôćĺ return ( 677Ôćĺ
678Ôćĺ {visibleTrains.map((train, i) => ( 679Ôćĺ 680Ôćĺ 681Ôćĺ {train.id} 682Ôćĺ 689Ôćĺ 690Ôćĺ 691Ôćĺ ))} 692Ôćĺ
693Ôćĺ ); 694Ôćĺ} 695Ôćĺ``` 696Ôćĺ 697Ôćĺ--- 698Ôćĺ 699Ôćĺ## Gotchas and Considerations 700Ôćĺ 701Ôćĺ### 1. CSS Import Order Matters 702Ôćĺ 703Ôćĺ```tsx 704Ôćĺ// CORRECT: PatternFly CSS before app styles 705Ôćĺimport '@patternfly/react-core/dist/styles/base.css'; 706Ôćĺimport './App.css'; // Your custom styles after 707Ôćĺ``` 708Ôćĺ 709Ôćĺ### 2. Chip Component Removed 710Ôćĺ 711ÔćĺPatternFly 6 replaced `Chip` with `Label`. If migrating from PatternFly 5: 712Ôćĺ```tsx 713Ôćĺ// Old (v5) 714ÔćĺFilter 715Ôćĺ 716Ôćĺ// New (v6) 717Ôćĺ 718Ôćĺ``` 719Ôćĺ 720Ôćĺ### 3. Text Component Renamed 721Ôćĺ 722Ôćĺ```tsx 723Ôćĺ// Old 724Ôćĺimport { Text, TextContent } from '@patternfly/react-core'; 725Ôćĺ 726Ôćĺ// New (v6) 727Ôćĺimport { Content } from '@patternfly/react-core'; 728Ôćĺ``` 729Ôćĺ 730Ôćĺ### 4. CSS Variable Prefix Changed 731Ôćĺ 732Ôćĺ```css 733Ôćĺ/* PatternFly 5 */ 734Ôćĺ--pf-v5-global--primary-color--100 735Ôćĺ 736Ôćĺ/* PatternFly 6 */ 737Ôćĺ--pf-v6-global--primary-color--100 738Ôćĺ``` 739Ôćĺ 740ÔćĺUse codemods for automatic updates: 741Ôćĺ```bash 742Ôćĺnpx @patternfly/pf-codemods@latest ./src --v6 --fix 743Ôćĺ``` 744Ôćĺ 745Ôćĺ### 5. Victory is Optional Peer Dependency 746Ôćĺ 747ÔćĺIf using charts, install Victory explicitly: 748Ôćĺ```bash 749Ôćĺbun add victory 750Ôćĺ``` 751Ôćĺ 752ÔćĺImport from victory subdirectory: 753Ôćĺ```tsx 754Ôćĺimport { Chart } from '@patternfly/react-charts/victory'; 755Ôćĺ``` 756Ôćĺ 757Ôćĺ### 6. Button aria-disabled Behavior Changed 758Ôćĺ 759ÔćĺTests may need updating if checking disabled state: 760Ôćĺ```tsx 761Ôćĺ// Test updates needed 762Ôćĺexpect(button).toHaveAttribute('aria-disabled', 'true'); 763Ôćĺ// instead of 764Ôćĺexpect(button).toBeDisabled(); 765Ôćĺ``` 766Ôćĺ 767Ôćĺ### 7. Rem Units for Breakpoints 768Ôćĺ 769ÔćĺPatternFly 6 uses rem instead of pixels for breakpoints: 770Ôćĺ```css 771Ôćĺ/* Convert: divide by 16 */ 772Ôćĺ/* 768px Ôćĺ 48rem */ 773Ôćĺ/* 992px Ôćĺ 62rem */ 774Ôćĺ/* 1200px Ôćĺ 75rem */ 775Ôćĺ``` 776Ôćĺ 777Ôćĺ### 8. No ButtonGroup Component 778Ôćĺ 779ÔćĺUse `ToolbarGroup` instead: 780Ôćĺ```tsx 781Ôćĺ// BlueprintJS 782Ôćĺ 783Ôćĺ 784Ôćĺ 785Ôćĺ 786Ôćĺ 787Ôćĺ// PatternFly 788Ôćĺ 789Ôćĺ 790Ôćĺ 791Ôćĺ 792Ôćĺ 793Ôćĺ 794Ôćĺ 795Ôćĺ 796Ôćĺ``` 797Ôćĺ 798Ôćĺ### 9. Full Height Canvas Setup 799Ôćĺ 800Ôćĺ```tsx 801Ôćĺ 810Ôćĺ 811Ôćĺ {/* Content */} 812Ôćĺ 813Ôćĺ 814Ôćĺ``` 815Ôćĺ 816Ôćĺ--- 817Ôćĺ 818Ôćĺ## Migration Checklist 819Ôćĺ 820Ôćĺ- [ ] Install PatternFly packages 821Ôćĺ- [ ] Add base CSS import (before app CSS) 822Ôćĺ- [ ] Replace Card imports 823Ôćĺ- [ ] Replace Tag with Label 824Ôćĺ- [ ] Replace ButtonGroup with ToolbarGroup 825Ôćĺ- [ ] Update Navbar to Masthead structure 826Ôćĺ- [ ] Replace Intent.* with variant/color props 827Ôćĺ- [ ] Run PatternFly codemods for automatic fixes 828Ôćĺ- [ ] Test all interactive components 829Ôćĺ- [ ] Verify canvas sizing in PatternFly layout 830Ôćĺ- [ ] Update any CSS variable references (v5 Ôćĺ v6) 831Ôćĺ 832Ôćĺ--- 833Ôćĺ 834Ôćĺ## Resources 835Ôćĺ 836Ôćĺ- [PatternFly Documentation](https://www.patternfly.org/) 837Ôćĺ- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react) 838Ôćĺ- [Upgrade Guide](https://www.patternfly.org/get-started/upgrade/) 839Ôćĺ- [Release Highlights](https://www.patternfly.org/get-started/release-highlights/) 840Ôćĺ- [React Training](https://www.patternfly.org/training/react-fundamentals-training/) 841Ôćĺ- [Topology Documentation](https://www.patternfly.org/topology/about-topology/) 842Ôćĺ- [Charts Documentation](https://www.patternfly.org/charts/about-charts/) 843Ôćĺ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware.
