# PatternFly v6 Research for MMS Migration > Research Date: 2026-01-05 > Sources: [PatternFly Official Documentation](https://www.patternfly.org/), [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react) --- ## Package Overview PatternFly is Red Hat's open source design system for enterprise applications. Version 6.4.0 is current (October 2025). ### Core Packages | Package | Purpose | Version | |---------|---------|---------| | `@patternfly/react-core` | Primary component library | 6.4.0 | | `@patternfly/react-table` | Table components | 6.x | | `@patternfly/react-charts` | Victory-based charting | 8.1.0 | | `@patternfly/react-topology` | Graph/network visualization | 6.3.0 | | `@patternfly/react-icons` | Icon components | 6.x | | `@patternfly/react-tokens` | CSS design tokens | 6.x | | `@patternfly/react-styles` | CSS styling utilities | 6.x | ### Installation ```bash # Core installation bun add @patternfly/react-core @patternfly/react-icons # For charts (optional) bun add @patternfly/react-charts victory # For topology (separate repo) bun add @patternfly/react-topology # For tables (if needed beyond react-core) bun add @patternfly/react-table ``` ### Required CSS Import ```typescript // Place BEFORE main app component import import '@patternfly/react-core/dist/styles/base.css'; // For dark mode charts support import '@patternfly/patternfly/patternfly-charts.css'; ``` --- ## React Version Compatibility **PatternFly 6 supports React 17, 18, AND 19** (Q2 2025 update). This is ideal for MMS because: - Current stack uses React 18 (required by BlueprintJS 6) - Migration can happen without React version change - Future upgrade path to React 19 is available --- ## Core Components for MMS ### 1. Page Layout System The `Page` component provides the main application structure: ```tsx import { Page, PageSection, PageSidebar, PageSidebarBody, Masthead, MastheadMain, MastheadBrand, MastheadLogo, MastheadContent, MastheadToggle, PageToggleButton } from '@patternfly/react-core'; function App() { return ( } sidebar={} isManagedSidebar // Auto manages open/close state > {/* Main canvas goes here */} ); } ``` **Key Props:** - `isManagedSidebar` - Automatic sidebar state management - `isContentFilled` - Sections fill available vertical space - `breadcrumb`, `horizontalSubnav` - Optional navigation elements ### 2. Card Component Direct replacement for BlueprintJS Card: ```tsx import { Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardBody, CardFooter, CardExpandableContent } from '@patternfly/react-core'; // Basic card Train TRAIN-001 Maintenance scheduled // Selectable card (for task selection) Task content // Expandable card Details Expanded content ``` **Variants:** - `isCompact` - Smaller padding - `isLarge` - Larger sizing - `isPlain` - No border/background - `variant="secondary"` - Secondary background color ### 3. Label Component (Tag Replacement) Replaces BlueprintJS Tag: ```tsx import { Label, LabelGroup } from '@patternfly/react-core'; // Basic labels with colors // Status labels (for LOD indicator) // With icon // Removable label // Label group ``` **Colors Available:** - Non-status: blue, teal, green, orange, purple, red, orange-red, grey, yellow - Status: success (green), warning (yellow), danger (red), info (purple) **Variants:** - `filled` - Solid background (default) - `outline` - Border only - `compact` - Reduced padding ### 4. Button Component ```tsx import { Button } from '@patternfly/react-core'; // Variants // Sizes // With loading state // Disabled states // Allows tooltip ``` ### 5. Toolbar (for controls area) Replaces ButtonGroup and control containers: ```tsx import { Toolbar, ToolbarContent, ToolbarGroup, ToolbarItem } from '@patternfly/react-core'; {/* Action buttons group */} {/* Label item */} {/* Separator */} {/* Pagination (right aligned) */} ``` ### 6. Navigation (Sidebar) ```tsx import { Nav, NavList, NavItem, NavExpandable } from '@patternfly/react-core';
Dashboard Planning TRAIN-001 TRAIN-002
``` ### 7. Drawer (Side Panel for Details) ```tsx import { Drawer, DrawerContent, DrawerPanelContent, DrawerHead, DrawerActions, DrawerCloseButton, DrawerPanelBody } from '@patternfly/react-core'; Task Details {/* Task detail content */} }> {/* Main content (canvas) */} ``` **Position Options:** - `end` (default) - Right side - `start` - Left side - `bottom` - Bottom panel --- ## Layout System ### Grid Layout ```tsx import { Grid, GridItem } from '@patternfly/react-core'; Sidebar Main Content // Responsive Content ``` ### PageSection Types ```tsx Main content Sub navigation Breadcrumbs Tab navigation Wizard content ``` --- ## WebGL Integration Strategy PatternFly components are standard React components - no special handling needed for WebGL/Three.js: ### Approach 1: PageSection Container ```tsx import { Page, PageSection } from '@patternfly/react-core'; import { Canvas } from '@react-three/fiber'; function App() { return ( ); } ``` ### Approach 2: Card Container (for embedded canvas) ```tsx {/* Three.js content */} ``` ### Approach 3: Drawer with Canvas ```tsx }>
``` **Key Considerations:** 1. Use `padding={{ default: 'noPadding' }}` on PageSection for full-bleed canvas 2. Set explicit heights or use flexbox/grid for proper canvas sizing 3. Canvas needs `position: absolute/relative` within container 4. Use `isFilled` prop for sections that should expand --- ## BlueprintJS to PatternFly Migration Map | BlueprintJS | PatternFly | Notes | |-------------|------------|-------| | `Card` | `Card` | Nearly identical API | | `Tag` | `Label` | Same concept, different name | | `Button` | `Button` | Similar variants | | `ButtonGroup` | `ToolbarGroup` | Use with Toolbar | | `Navbar` | `Masthead` | Different structure | | `Intent.PRIMARY` | `variant="primary"` | Color as variant | | `Intent.DANGER` | `variant="danger"` or `isDanger` | | | `Intent.WARNING` | `variant="warning"` | | | `Intent.SUCCESS` | `color="green"` (Label) | | | `Collapse` | `CardExpandableContent` | Or `Expandable` | | `Drawer` | `Drawer` | Similar concept | | `Tabs` | `Tabs` | Available in react-core | | `Spinner` | `Spinner` | Available | | `ProgressBar` | `Progress` | Available | ### Intent Colors Mapping ```tsx // BlueprintJS Blue Red Orange Green // PatternFly equivalent // or status="danger" // or status="warning" // or status="success" ``` ### Button Migration ```tsx // BlueprintJS // PatternFly ``` --- ## Charts Integration PatternFly uses Victory library for charts: ```tsx import { Chart, ChartArea, ChartAxis, ChartLine } from '@patternfly/react-charts/victory'; // Basic line chart ``` **Note:** Victory is optional peer dependency in v6. Import from `@patternfly/react-charts/victory`. --- ## Topology Component For network/dependency visualization (future MMS feature): ```tsx import { Visualization, VisualizationProvider, VisualizationSurface, Model, GRAPH_LAYOUT_FORCE } from '@patternfly/react-topology'; const model: Model = { nodes: [...], edges: [...], graph: { id: 'graph', type: 'graph', layout: GRAPH_LAYOUT_FORCE } }; ``` --- ## Code Examples ### Complete App Shell ```tsx import React, { useState } from 'react'; import '@patternfly/react-core/dist/styles/base.css'; import { Page, PageSection, PageSidebar, PageSidebarBody, Masthead, MastheadMain, MastheadBrand, MastheadLogo, MastheadContent, MastheadToggle, PageToggleButton, Toolbar, ToolbarContent, ToolbarGroup, ToolbarItem, Button, Label, Nav, NavList, NavItem, Drawer, DrawerContent, DrawerPanelContent, DrawerHead, DrawerActions, DrawerCloseButton, DrawerPanelBody } from '@patternfly/react-core'; import { BarsIcon } from '@patternfly/react-icons'; import { Canvas } from '@react-three/fiber'; function App() { const [sidebarOpen, setSidebarOpen] = useState(true); const [detailsOpen, setDetailsOpen] = useState(false); const [lod, setLod] = useState<'MACRO' | 'MESO' | 'MICRO'>('MESO'); const masthead = ( setSidebarOpen(!sidebarOpen)} > MMS Planning ); const sidebar = (
Planning Reports Settings
); const detailsPanel = ( Task Details setDetailsOpen(false)} /> {/* Task details content */} ); return (
{/* R3F content */}
); } export default App; ``` ### Train Sidebar Component ```tsx import { Card, CardBody, Label } from '@patternfly/react-core'; interface TrainSidebarProps { trains: Train[]; visibleRange: [number, number]; } export function TrainSidebar({ trains, visibleRange }: TrainSidebarProps) { const [start, end] = visibleRange; const visibleTrains = trains.slice(start, end); return (
{visibleTrains.map((train, i) => ( {train.id} ))}
); } ``` --- ## Gotchas and Considerations ### 1. CSS Import Order Matters ```tsx // CORRECT: PatternFly CSS before app styles import '@patternfly/react-core/dist/styles/base.css'; import './App.css'; // Your custom styles after ``` ### 2. Chip Component Removed PatternFly 6 replaced `Chip` with `Label`. If migrating from PatternFly 5: ```tsx // Old (v5) Filter // New (v6) ``` ### 3. Text Component Renamed ```tsx // Old import { Text, TextContent } from '@patternfly/react-core'; // New (v6) import { Content } from '@patternfly/react-core'; ``` ### 4. CSS Variable Prefix Changed ```css /* PatternFly 5 */ --pf-v5-global--primary-color--100 /* PatternFly 6 */ --pf-v6-global--primary-color--100 ``` Use codemods for automatic updates: ```bash npx @patternfly/pf-codemods@latest ./src --v6 --fix ``` ### 5. Victory is Optional Peer Dependency If using charts, install Victory explicitly: ```bash bun add victory ``` Import from victory subdirectory: ```tsx import { Chart } from '@patternfly/react-charts/victory'; ``` ### 6. Button aria-disabled Behavior Changed Tests may need updating if checking disabled state: ```tsx // Test updates needed expect(button).toHaveAttribute('aria-disabled', 'true'); // instead of expect(button).toBeDisabled(); ``` ### 7. Rem Units for Breakpoints PatternFly 6 uses rem instead of pixels for breakpoints: ```css /* Convert: divide by 16 */ /* 768px Ôćĺ 48rem */ /* 992px Ôćĺ 62rem */ /* 1200px Ôćĺ 75rem */ ``` ### 8. No ButtonGroup Component Use `ToolbarGroup` instead: ```tsx // BlueprintJS // PatternFly ``` ### 9. Full Height Canvas Setup ```tsx {/* Content */} ``` --- ## Migration Checklist - [ ] Install PatternFly packages - [ ] Add base CSS import (before app CSS) - [ ] Replace Card imports - [ ] Replace Tag with Label - [ ] Replace ButtonGroup with ToolbarGroup - [ ] Update Navbar to Masthead structure - [ ] Replace Intent.* with variant/color props - [ ] Run PatternFly codemods for automatic fixes - [ ] Test all interactive components - [ ] Verify canvas sizing in PatternFly layout - [ ] Update any CSS variable references (v5 Ôćĺ v6) --- ## Resources - [PatternFly Documentation](https://www.patternfly.org/) - [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react) - [Upgrade Guide](https://www.patternfly.org/get-started/upgrade/) - [Release Highlights](https://www.patternfly.org/get-started/release-highlights/) - [React Training](https://www.patternfly.org/training/react-fundamentals-training/) - [Topology Documentation](https://www.patternfly.org/topology/about-topology/) - [Charts Documentation](https://www.patternfly.org/charts/about-charts/)

1Ôćĺ# MMS V1.0 GENESIS ÔÇö Master Orchestration Prompt
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ> **Purpose**: Consolidate 6 projects into one clean V1.0 baseline.
     4Ôćĺ> **Method**: Parallel extraction Ôćĺ Validation Ôćĺ Synthesis Ôćĺ Implementation.
     5Ôćĺ> **Resources**: UNLIMITED. Quality is the only metric.
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ---
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ## I. MISSION
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ**Consolidate all knowledge from 6 existing MMS projects into a single, clean V1.0 codebase.**
    12Ôćĺ
    13ÔćĺThe projects contain years of experimentation, multiple tech stack attempts, and distributed documentation. Your job is to:
    14Ôćĺ
    15Ôćĺ1. **EXTRACT** all valuable knowledge (domain, technical, UX)
    16Ôćĺ2. **VALIDATE** what actually works vs what's only documented
    17Ôćĺ3. **SYNTHESIZE** into one authoritative specification
    18Ôćĺ4. **IMPLEMENT** a clean baseline with new stack
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺ**Client**: Dubai Metro / Keolis MHI
    21Ôćĺ**Product**: Fleet maintenance planning visualization (128 trains, 2 depots, 12-year contract)
    22Ôćĺ**Core UX**: Semantic Zoom (Google Earth for time) ÔÇö pan through years, zoom into days
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ---
    25Ôćĺ
    26Ôćĺ## II. KNOWLEDGE SOURCES
    27Ôćĺ
    28Ôćĺ```
    29Ôćĺ~/antonio/mms/
    30ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ system/          # PRIMARY ÔÇö Production app (React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS)
    31ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ deck/            # POC ÔÇö deck.gl experiments, harness research
    32ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ MFC/             # Full-stack ÔÇö Next.js + Python OR-Tools solver
    33ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ projects/        # Historical ÔÇö Past attempts (4 subdirs)
    34ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ design/          # Visual ÔÇö Reference mockups (9 images)
    35ÔćĺÔööÔöÇÔöÇ system_started/  # Template ÔÇö Bootstrap structure
    36Ôćĺ```
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ**Key files to extract from**:
    39Ôćĺ- `system/docs/*.md` ÔÇö Domain specs, QA protocols, research
    40Ôćĺ- `system/apps/canvas/` ÔÇö Working LOD implementation
    41Ôćĺ- `system/.harness/` ÔÇö Decision history, learnings
    42Ôćĺ- `MFC/` ÔÇö Solver logic, mathematical formulas
    43Ôćĺ- `deck/` ÔÇö Alternative approaches, harness improvements
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ---
    46Ôćĺ
    47Ôćĺ## III. TARGET STATE
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ```
    50Ôćĺ~/antonio/mms/system/  (V1.0)
    51ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ apps/canvas/       # PatternFly v6 + Canvas (R3F or alternative)
    52ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ docs/SPECIFICATION.md  # Single source (~3000-4000 lines)
    53ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ .harness/          # Clean V1.0 feature tracking
    54ÔćĺÔööÔöÇÔöÇ CLAUDE.md          # Updated conventions
    55Ôćĺ```
    56Ôćĺ
    57Ôćĺ**Stack Migration**:
    58Ôćĺ| From | To |
    59Ôćĺ|------|-----|
    60Ôćĺ| BlueprintJS 6 | PatternFly v6 |
    61Ôćĺ| @zazcart/toolkit | @belt/tools |
    62Ôćĺ| packages/toolkit/ | DELETE (use npm) |
    63Ôćĺ
    64Ôćĺ---
    65Ôćĺ
    66Ôćĺ## IV. CORE HEURISTICS
    67Ôćĺ
    68Ôćĺ```
    69Ôćĺ1. TRUST NOTHING, VERIFY EVERYTHING
    70Ôćĺ   Documentation claims features work. Code may not run.
    71Ôćĺ   Categorize: VERIFIED (executed) | CLAIMED (documented) | DEPRECATED
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ2. PARALLEL WIDE, SEQUENTIAL DEEP
    74Ôćĺ   Extraction: Many agents, maximum coverage
    75Ôćĺ   Synthesis: One agent, full coherence
    76Ôćĺ
    77Ôćĺ3. DISK OVER MEMORY
    78Ôćĺ   Context rots. Write to _migration_memory/
    79Ôćĺ   Sub-agents return condensed summaries (1-2k tokens)
    80Ôćĺ
    81Ôćĺ4. ONE FEATURE, ONE SESSION
    82Ôćĺ   Incremental progress. Verify before proceeding.
    83Ôćĺ   Leave codebase mergeable.
    84Ôćĺ
    85Ôćĺ5. DECISIONS THROUGH DEBATE
    86Ôćĺ   Major choices: spawn SIMPLE vs COMPLETE vs PERF advocates
    87Ôćĺ   Synthesizer produces consensus with evidence
    88Ôćĺ```
    89Ôćĺ
    90Ôćĺ---
    91Ôćĺ
    92Ôćĺ## V. EXECUTION PHASES
    93Ôćĺ
    94Ôćĺ### PHASE 1: Territory Mapping
    95Ôćĺ**Agents**: 1
    96Ôćĺ**Output**: `_migration_memory/00_TERRITORY_MAP.md`
    97Ôćĺ
    98ÔćĺScan all 6 nodes. Count files, identify key assets, check git history. Do NOT read content yet.
    99Ôćĺ
   100Ôćĺ---
   101Ôćĺ
   102Ôćĺ### PHASE 2: Parallel Knowledge Extraction
   103Ôćĺ**Agents**: 6-8 parallel
   104Ôćĺ**Outputs**: `_migration_memory/01_*.md` through `08_*.md`
   105Ôćĺ
   106Ôćĺ| Agent | Target | Focus |
   107Ôćĺ|-------|--------|-------|
   108Ôćĺ| EXT-CANVAS | system/apps/canvas/ | What renders, what performs |
   109Ôćĺ| EXT-DOCS | system/docs/ | Domain knowledge, QA protocols |
   110Ôćĺ| EXT-HARNESS | system/.harness/ + git | Decision history, learnings |
   111Ôćĺ| EXT-MFC | MFC/ | Solver logic, math formulas |
   112Ôćĺ| EXT-DECK | deck/ | Alternative approaches |
   113Ôćĺ| EXT-PROJECTS | projects/ | Historical experiments |
   114Ôćĺ| EXT-DESIGN | design/ | Visual specifications |
   115Ôćĺ
   116Ôćĺ**HEVA Protocol Template** (for all extraction agents):
   117Ôćĺ```
   118ÔćĺFor each finding:
   119Ôćĺ1. HYPOTHESIS: "I believe [X] because..."
   120Ôćĺ2. EVIDENCE: "Found in [file:line] or [commit:hash]..."
   121Ôćĺ3. VERIFICATION: "Confirmed by [code executed / test passed / screenshot]..."
   122Ôćĺ4. ARTIFACT: Written to _migration_memory/[file].md
   123Ôćĺ
   124ÔćĺCategories:
   125Ôćĺ- VERIFIED: Code ran, test passed
   126Ôćĺ- CLAIMED: Documented but not verified
   127Ôćĺ- UNIQUE_KNOWLEDGE: Worth preserving
   128Ôćĺ- DEPRECATED: Do not carry forward
   129Ôćĺ
   130ÔćĺBE THOROUGH. Unlimited tokens. Miss nothing.
   131Ôćĺ```
   132Ôćĺ
   133Ôćĺ---
   134Ôćĺ
   135Ôćĺ### PHASE 3: Technology Research
   136Ôćĺ**Agents**: 3 parallel
   137Ôćĺ**Outputs**: `_migration_memory/10_*.md`
   138Ôćĺ
   139Ôćĺ| Agent | Focus |
   140Ôćĺ|-------|-------|
   141Ôćĺ| TECH-PF | PatternFly v6 ÔÇö components, integration, migration path |
   142Ôćĺ| TECH-BELT | @belt/tools ÔÇö API, patterns, migration from toolkit |
   143Ôćĺ| TECH-CANVAS | Canvas tech comparison ÔÇö R3F vs deck.gl vs alternatives |
   144Ôćĺ
   145Ôćĺ**Note**: Research happens HERE, not before. Agents discover what's needed.
   146Ôćĺ
   147Ôćĺ---
   148Ôćĺ
   149Ôćĺ### PHASE 4: Cross-Validation
   150Ôćĺ**Agents**: 3-4 parallel
   151Ôćĺ**Outputs**: `_migration_memory/20_*.md`
   152Ôćĺ
   153ÔćĺCross-reference extraction artifacts. Identify:
   154Ôćĺ- **CONFLICTS**: Different sources disagree
   155Ôćĺ- **GAPS**: Missing knowledge
   156Ôćĺ- **VERIFIED**: Multiple sources + code confirms
   157Ôćĺ
   158Ôćĺ---
   159Ôćĺ
   160Ôćĺ### PHASE 5: Decision Debates
   161Ôćĺ**Agents**: 4 (3 advocates + 1 synthesizer)
   162Ôćĺ**Output**: `_migration_memory/35_DECISIONS.md`
   163Ôćĺ
   164Ôćĺ**Topics**: Canvas technology, spec structure, V1.0 scope, PatternFly integration
   165Ôćĺ
   166Ôćĺ**Protocol**:
   167Ôćĺ1. Position papers (500 words each)
   168Ôćĺ2. Cross-examination
   169Ôćĺ3. Synthesis with evidence
   170Ôćĺ
   171Ôćĺ---
   172Ôćĺ
   173Ôćĺ### PHASE 6: Specification Writing
   174Ôćĺ**Agents**: 3 writers + 1 editor
   175Ôćĺ**Output**: `docs/SPECIFICATION.md`
   176Ôćĺ
   177ÔćĺWrite the FINAL spec. No migration notes. No alternatives. Only decided approaches.
   178Ôćĺ
   179ÔćĺInclude: Domain (Dubai Metro context), Architecture, UX, Math formulas, Demo strategy.
   180Ôćĺ
   181Ôćĺ---
   182Ôćĺ
   183Ôćĺ### PHASE 7: Repository Cleanup
   184Ôćĺ**Agent**: 1
   185Ôćĺ**Actions**:
   186Ôćĺ1. Backup branch
   187Ôćĺ2. Delete deprecated: `packages/`, old docs
   188Ôćĺ3. Update dependencies (PatternFly v6, @belt/tools)
   189Ôćĺ4. Archive old harness, create new
   190Ôćĺ5. Delete `_migration_memory/`
   191Ôćĺ6. Commit clean baseline
   192Ôćĺ
   193Ôćĺ---
   194Ôćĺ
   195Ôćĺ### PHASE 8: Implementation
   196Ôćĺ**Protocol**: Anthropic's two-agent architecture
   197Ôćĺ
   198Ôćĺ**Harness Structure** (JSON, not Markdown):
   199Ôćĺ```json
   200Ôćĺ// features.json
   201Ôćĺ{
   202Ôćĺ  "version": "1.0.0",
   203Ôćĺ  "features": [
   204Ôćĺ    {
   205Ôćĺ      "id": "F001",
   206Ôćĺ      "category": "foundation",
   207Ôćĺ      "description": "Vite + React 18 + PatternFly v6 scaffold",
   208Ôćĺ      "verification": ["npm run dev works", "PatternFly renders"],
   209Ôćĺ      "passes": false
   210Ôćĺ    }
   211Ôćĺ  ]
   212Ôćĺ}
   213Ôćĺ```
   214Ôćĺ
   215Ôćĺ**Feature Phases**:
   216Ôćĺ- **A (F001-F010)**: Canvas Foundation ÔÇö scaffold, Storybook, state, LOD layers, pan/zoom, 60fps
   217Ôćĺ- **B (F011-F015)**: UI Integration ÔÇö sidebar, timeline, minimap, drawer, keyboard
   218Ôćĺ- **C (F016-F020)**: Solver ÔÇö mock API, real solver, allocation, What-If, demo mode
   219Ôćĺ
   220Ôćĺ**Session Protocol** (every session):
   221Ôćĺ```
   222Ôćĺ1. RECOVER: cat progress.txt | tail -100 && git log --oneline -15
   223Ôćĺ2. SELECT: Find ONE incomplete feature in features.json
   224Ôćĺ3. VERIFY: Confirm previous feature still works
   225Ôćĺ4. IMPLEMENT: Code ONE feature only
   226Ôćĺ5. TEST: Visual + performance verification
   227Ôćĺ6. COMMIT: Atomic, descriptive message
   228Ôćĺ7. UPDATE: Mark passes:true, append to progress.txt
   229Ôćĺ8. STOP: Leave codebase mergeable
   230Ôćĺ```
   231Ôćĺ
   232Ôćĺ---
   233Ôćĺ
   234Ôćĺ## VI. PRESERVED KNOWLEDGE
   235Ôćĺ

# MMS V1.0 GENESIS ÔÇö Master Orchestration Prompt

> **Purpose**: Consolidate 6 projects into one clean V1.0 baseline.
> **Method**: Parallel extraction Ôćĺ Validation Ôćĺ Synthesis Ôćĺ Implementation.
> **Resources**: UNLIMITED. Quality is the only metric.

---

## I. MISSION

**Consolidate all knowledge from 6 existing MMS projects into a single, clean V1.0 codebase.**

The projects contain years of experimentation, multiple tech stack attempts, and distributed documentation. Your job is to:

1. **EXTRACT** all valuable knowledge (domain, technical, UX)
2. **VALIDATE** what actually works vs what's only documented
3. **SYNTHESIZE** into one authoritative specification
4. **IMPLEMENT** a clean baseline with new stack

**Client**: Dubai Metro / Keolis MHI
**Product**: Fleet maintenance planning visualization (128 trains, 2 depots, 12-year contract)
**Core UX**: Semantic Zoom (Google Earth for time) ÔÇö pan through years, zoom into days

---

## II. KNOWLEDGE SOURCES

```
~/antonio/mms/
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ system/          # PRIMARY ÔÇö Production app (React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS)
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ deck/            # POC ÔÇö deck.gl experiments, harness research
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ MFC/             # Full-stack ÔÇö Next.js + Python OR-Tools solver
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ projects/        # Historical ÔÇö Past attempts (4 subdirs)
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ design/          # Visual ÔÇö Reference mockups (9 images)
ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ system_started/  # Template ÔÇö Bootstrap structure
```

**Key files to extract from**:
- `system/docs/*.md` ÔÇö Domain specs, QA protocols, research
- `system/apps/canvas/` ÔÇö Working LOD implementation
- `system/.harness/` ÔÇö Decision history, learnings
- `MFC/` ÔÇö Solver logic, mathematical formulas
- `deck/` ÔÇö Alternative approaches, harness improvements

---

## III. TARGET STATE

```
~/antonio/mms/system/  (V1.0)
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ apps/canvas/       # PatternFly v6 + Canvas (R3F or alternative)
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ docs/SPECIFICATION.md  # Single source (~3000-4000 lines)
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ .harness/          # Clean V1.0 feature tracking
ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ CLAUDE.md          # Updated conventions
```

**Stack Migration**:
| From | To |
|------|-----|
| BlueprintJS 6 | PatternFly v6 |
| @zazcart/toolkit | @belt/tools |
| packages/toolkit/ | DELETE (use npm) |

---

## IV. CORE HEURISTICS

```
1. TRUST NOTHING, VERIFY EVERYTHING
   Documentation claims features work. Code may not run.
   Categorize: VERIFIED (executed) | CLAIMED (documented) | DEPRECATED

2. PARALLEL WIDE, SEQUENTIAL DEEP
   Extraction: Many agents, maximum coverage
   Synthesis: One agent, full coherence

3. DISK OVER MEMORY
   Context rots. Write to _migration_memory/
   Sub-agents return condensed summaries (1-2k tokens)

4. ONE FEATURE, ONE SESSION
   Incremental progress. Verify before proceeding.
   Leave codebase mergeable.

5. DECISIONS THROUGH DEBATE
   Major choices: spawn SIMPLE vs COMPLETE vs PERF advocates
   Synthesizer produces consensus with evidence
```

---

## V. EXECUTION PHASES

### PHASE 1: Territory Mapping
**Agents**: 1
**Output**: `_migration_memory/00_TERRITORY_MAP.md`

Scan all 6 nodes. Count files, identify key assets, check git history. Do NOT read content yet.

---

### PHASE 2: Parallel Knowledge Extraction
**Agents**: 6-8 parallel
**Outputs**: `_migration_memory/01_*.md` through `08_*.md`

| Agent | Target | Focus |
|-------|--------|-------|
| EXT-CANVAS | system/apps/canvas/ | What renders, what performs |
| EXT-DOCS | system/docs/ | Domain knowledge, QA protocols |
| EXT-HARNESS | system/.harness/ + git | Decision history, learnings |
| EXT-MFC | MFC/ | Solver logic, math formulas |
| EXT-DECK | deck/ | Alternative approaches |
| EXT-PROJECTS | projects/ | Historical experiments |
| EXT-DESIGN | design/ | Visual specifications |

**HEVA Protocol Template** (for all extraction agents):
```
For each finding:
1. HYPOTHESIS: "I believe [X] because..."
2. EVIDENCE: "Found in [file:line] or [commit:hash]..."
3. VERIFICATION: "Confirmed by [code executed / test passed / screenshot]..."
4. ARTIFACT: Written to _migration_memory/[file].md

Categories:
- VERIFIED: Code ran, test passed
- CLAIMED: Documented but not verified
- UNIQUE_KNOWLEDGE: Worth preserving
- DEPRECATED: Do not carry forward

BE THOROUGH. Unlimited tokens. Miss nothing.
```

---

### PHASE 3: Technology Research
**Agents**: 3 parallel
**Outputs**: `_migration_memory/10_*.md`

| Agent | Focus |
|-------|-------|
| TECH-PF | PatternFly v6 ÔÇö components, integration, migration path |
| TECH-BELT | @belt/tools ÔÇö API, patterns, migration from toolkit |
| TECH-CANVAS | Canvas tech comparison ÔÇö R3F vs deck.gl vs alternatives |

**Note**: Research happens HERE, not before. Agents discover what's needed.

---

### PHASE 4: Cross-Validation
**Agents**: 3-4 parallel
**Outputs**: `_migration_memory/20_*.md`

Cross-reference extraction artifacts. Identify:
- **CONFLICTS**: Different sources disagree
- **GAPS**: Missing knowledge
- **VERIFIED**: Multiple sources + code confirms

---

### PHASE 5: Decision Debates
**Agents**: 4 (3 advocates + 1 synthesizer)
**Output**: `_migration_memory/35_DECISIONS.md`

**Topics**: Canvas technology, spec structure, V1.0 scope, PatternFly integration

**Protocol**:
1. Position papers (500 words each)
2. Cross-examination
3. Synthesis with evidence

---

### PHASE 6: Specification Writing
**Agents**: 3 writers + 1 editor
**Output**: `docs/SPECIFICATION.md`

Write the FINAL spec. No migration notes. No alternatives. Only decided approaches.

Include: Domain (Dubai Metro context), Architecture, UX, Math formulas, Demo strategy.

---

### PHASE 7: Repository Cleanup
**Agent**: 1
**Actions**:
1. Backup branch
2. Delete deprecated: `packages/`, old docs
3. Update dependencies (PatternFly v6, @belt/tools)
4. Archive old harness, create new
5. Delete `_migration_memory/`
6. Commit clean baseline

---

### PHASE 8: Implementation
**Protocol**: Anthropic's two-agent architecture

**Harness Structure** (JSON, not Markdown):
```json
// features.json
{
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "F001",
      "category": "foundation",
      "description": "Vite + React 18 + PatternFly v6 scaffold",
      "verification": ["npm run dev works", "PatternFly renders"],
      "passes": false
    }
  ]
}
```

**Feature Phases**:
- **A (F001-F010)**: Canvas Foundation ÔÇö scaffold, Storybook, state, LOD layers, pan/zoom, 60fps
- **B (F011-F015)**: UI Integration ÔÇö sidebar, timeline, minimap, drawer, keyboard
- **C (F016-F020)**: Solver ÔÇö mock API, real solver, allocation, What-If, demo mode

**Session Protocol** (every session):
```
1. RECOVER: cat progress.txt | tail -100 && git log --oneline -15
2. SELECT: Find ONE incomplete feature in features.json
3. VERIFY: Confirm previous feature still works
4. IMPLEMENT: Code ONE feature only
5. TEST: Visual + performance verification
6. COMMIT: Atomic, descriptive message
7. UPDATE: Mark passes:true, append to progress.txt
8. STOP: Leave codebase mergeable
```

---

## VI. PRESERVED KNOWLEDGE

### Domain (Dubai Metro)
- **Client**: Keolis MHI (Dubai Metro)
- **Fleets**: Alstom (79, Red Line, Al Rashidiya depot) + Kinki Sharyo (49, Green Line, Jebel Ali depot)
- **Total**: 128 trains, 9 tracks, 5,200 weekly man-hours
- **Contract**: 12 years
- **Maintenance**: A-Check (10k km, 4h) Ôćĺ B-Check (50k, 16h) Ôćĺ C-Check (200k, 72h) Ôćĺ D-Overhaul (800k, 480h)

### Technical (from harness history)
- **STATE-ARCH-001**: TypedArrays + SoA pattern for 60fps (GC is the bottleneck)
- **TOOLKIT-PERF-001**: 7 GC optimization fixes documented
- **LOD**: Macro (<0.5x) | Meso (0.5-2x) | Micro (>2x) with crossfade
- **RBush**: O(log n) spatial index for viewport culling

### KPI Formulas (PRESERVE)
```javascript
kmVariance = (stddev(currentKms) / mean(currentKms)) * 100  // PRIMARY
availability = (fitTrains / totalTrains) * 100
utilization = (scheduledTracks / availableTracks) * 100
trainDelta = ((trainKm - fleetAvgKm) / fleetAvgKm) * 100
```

### Competitive Context
| Competitor | Gap MMS Fills |
|------------|---------------|
| DELMIA Quintiq | Too complex, slow time-to-value |
| Railnova | Basic optimization only |
| SignatureRail | Black-box decisions, no transparency |

### Demo Strategy (12 min)
| Time | Moment | Wow Factor |
|------|--------|------------|
| 0:30-2:00 | Variance Discovery | "Your fleet is 18% out of balance" |
| 2:00-5:00 | Justification Card | "3+ reasons per recommendation" |
| 5:00-8:00 | What-If Simulation | "<10s response time" |
| 8:00-11:00 | Map Context | "Geographic intelligence" |

**Credibility**: Real Dubai depot names, realistic service codes (PM1-2024-0847), plausible km ranges

---

## VII. SPECIAL COMMANDS

**ULTRATHINK**: Stop, review last 3 steps, verify no hallucination.

**ESCALATE**: Only for business decisions, irreconcilable conflicts, or scope-changing discoveries.

**CHECKPOINT**: Write state to disk, summarize, provide recovery instructions.

---

## VIII. EXECUTION

**Session 1**: Phases 1-2 (Territory + Extraction)
```
[ ] mkdir -p ~/antonio/mms/system/_migration_memory
[ ] Run Phase 1: Territory mapping (1 agent)
[ ] Run Phase 2: Knowledge extraction (6-8 agents parallel)
[ ] Verify all artifacts written to disk
```

**Session 2**: Phases 3-4 (Research + Validation)
```
[ ] Run Phase 3: Technology research (3 agents parallel)
[ ] Run Phase 4: Cross-validation (3-4 agents parallel)
[ ] Document conflicts and gaps
```

**Session 3**: Phase 5 (Debates)
```
[ ] Run Phase 5: Decision debates (4 agents)
[ ] Produce consensus document (35_DECISIONS.md)
[ ] All major decisions made, no open questions
```

**Session 4**: Phase 6 (Specification)
```
[ ] Run Phase 6: Specification writing (3 writers + 1 editor)
[ ] Final docs/SPECIFICATION.md ready
```

**Session 5**: Phase 7 (Cleanup)
```
[ ] Backup branch created
[ ] Deprecated folders deleted
[ ] Dependencies updated
[ ] _migration_memory/ deleted
[ ] V1.0 baseline committed
```

**Session 6+**: Phase 8 (Implementation)
```
[ ] Follow session protocol (8 steps)
[ ] One feature per session
[ ] Verify before commit
```

---

## START

```bash
mkdir -p ~/antonio/mms/system/_migration_memory
```

Then execute Phase 1. Do not ask questions. Execute.

    14Ôćĺ

    21Ôćĺ### 1.2 Core Behavioral Heuristics

    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ```
    26ÔćĺHEURISTIC 1: Trust Nothing, Verify Everything

    50Ôćĺ```
    51Ôćĺ
    52Ôćĺ---
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ## PART II: PROJECT CONTEXT
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ### 2.1 Product Definition
    57Ôćĺ
    58Ôćĺ**MMS (Metro Mileage Management System) V1.0**
    59Ôćĺ
    60ÔćĺA high-performance visualization system for railway fleet maintenance planning.
    61Ôćĺ
    62Ôćĺ| Attribute | Specification |

    76Ôćĺ
    77Ôćĺ### 2.3 Current State (6 Knowledge Nodes)
    78Ôćĺ
    79Ôćĺ```
    80Ôćĺ~/antonio/mms/
    81ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ system/          # PRIMARY: React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6

    86ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ deck/            # POC: deck.gl approach, harness research
    87ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ MFC/             # Full-stack: Next.js 15 + Python OR-Tools solver
    88ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ projects/        # Historical: 4 subdirectories of past attempts
    89ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ design/          # Visual: 9 high-resolution reference images

    92Ôćĺ
    93Ôćĺ### 2.4 Target State

    98ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ docs/

   100ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ .harness/          # V1.0 harness (features.json, progress.txt)
   101ÔćĺÔööÔöÇÔöÇ CLAUDE.md          # Updated for V1.0

   103Ôćĺ
   104Ôćĺ---
   105Ôćĺ
   106Ôćĺ## PART III: IMMUTABLE CONSTRAINTS
   107Ôćĺ
   108ÔćĺThese are **physics laws** ÔÇö cannot be violated under any circumstance:
   109Ôćĺ
   110Ôćĺ| Legacy | V1.0 | Rule |
   111Ôćĺ|--------|------|------|

   121Ôćĺ## PART IV: EXECUTION PHASES

   126Ôćĺ**Agents**: 1

   509Ôćĺ## PART V: PRESERVED KNOWLEDGE
   510Ôćĺ
   511Ôćĺ### 5.1 Critical Technical Learnings
   512Ôćĺ
   513Ôćĺ**STATE-ARCH-001: TypedArrays + SoA for 60fps**
   514Ôćĺ```javascript
   515Ôćĺ// Structure of Arrays pattern
   516Ôćĺconst trainData = {
   517Ôćĺ  ids: new Uint32Array(MAX_TRAINS),
   518Ôćĺ  x: new Float32Array(MAX_TRAINS),
   519Ôćĺ  y: new Float32Array(MAX_TRAINS),
   520Ôćĺ  km: new Float32Array(MAX_TRAINS),
   521Ôćĺ  status: new Uint8Array(MAX_TRAINS),
   522Ôćĺ  count: 0
   523Ôćĺ};
   524Ôćĺ
   525Ôćĺ// Update without GC pressure
   526Ôćĺfunction updateTrain(index, x, y, km) {
   527Ôćĺ  trainData.x[index] = x;
   528Ôćĺ  trainData.y[index] = y;
   529Ôćĺ  trainData.km[index] = km;
   530Ôćĺ  // No object allocation = no GC = stable 60fps
   531Ôćĺ}
   532Ôćĺ```
   533Ôćĺ
   534Ôćĺ**TOOLKIT-PERF-001: 7 GC Optimization Fixes**
   535Ôćĺ1. Copy array before iteration (unsafe unsubscribe)
   536Ôćĺ2. Skip validation in production (hot path)
   537Ôćĺ3. Object.assign instead of reduceObject (O(N) vs O(N┬▓))
   538Ôćĺ4. `trackMetadata: false` option (eliminate GC thrashing)
   539Ôćĺ5. Clean object structure (prevent V8 de-optimization)
   540Ôćĺ6. Direct assignment (avoid double proxying)
   541Ôćĺ7. Safe error handling in devLog
   542Ôćĺ
   543Ôćĺ**LOD System Thresholds**:
   544Ôćĺ```javascript
   545Ôćĺconst LOD_THRESHOLDS = {
   546Ôćĺ  MACRO: { max: 0.5 },   // zoom < 0.5x: Aggregated heatmap
   547Ôćĺ  MESO: { min: 0.5, max: 2.0 },  // 0.5x-2x: Task blocks
   548Ôćĺ  MICRO: { min: 2.0 }    // zoom > 2x: Detail cards
   549Ôćĺ};
   550Ôćĺ
   551Ôćĺconst CROSSFADE_WIDTH = 0.1; // Smooth transition zone
   552Ôćĺ```
   553Ôćĺ
   554Ôćĺ**RBush Spatial Index**: O(log n) viewport culling
   555Ôćĺ```javascript
   556Ôćĺimport RBush from 'rbush';
   557Ôćĺconst tree = new RBush();
   558Ôćĺtree.load(tasks.map(t => ({
   559Ôćĺ  minX: t.x, minY: t.y,
   560Ôćĺ  maxX: t.x + t.width, maxY: t.y + t.height,
   561Ôćĺ  task: t
   562Ôćĺ})));
   563Ôćĺconst visible = tree.search(viewport);
   564Ôćĺ```
   565Ôćĺ
   566Ôćĺ### 5.2 Domain Knowledge
   567Ôćĺ
   568Ôćĺ**ECM 2019/779 Functions**:
   569Ôćĺ| Function | Name | MMS Feature |
   570Ôćĺ|----------|------|-------------|
   571Ôćĺ| F1 | Management | Audit logs, compliance dashboard |
   572Ôćĺ| F2 | Maintenance Development | Rule configuration, threshold settings |
   573Ôćĺ| F3 | Fleet Maintenance Management | Km balancing, scheduling optimization |
   574Ôćĺ| F4 | Maintenance Delivery | Work order tracking, completion status |
   575Ôćĺ
   576Ôćĺ**RAMS Metrics (EN 50126-1)**:
   577Ôćĺ```javascript
   578Ôćĺconst RAMS = {
   579Ôćĺ  reliability: {
   580Ôćĺ    mtbf: totalOperatingHours / failureCount,
   581Ôćĺ    failureRate: failureCount / totalOperatingHours
   582Ôćĺ  },
   583Ôćĺ  availability: {
   584Ôćĺ    operational: (totalTime - downtimeHours) / totalTime * 100,
   585Ôćĺ    inherent: mtbf / (mtbf + mttr)
   586Ôćĺ  },
   587Ôćĺ  maintainability: {
   588Ôćĺ    mttr: totalRepairHours / repairCount,
   589Ôćĺ    completionRate: completedWorkOrders / totalWorkOrders * 100
   590Ôćĺ  },
   591Ôćĺ  safety: {
   592Ôćĺ    hazardRate: safetyIncidents / operatingHours,
   593Ôćĺ    severityIndex: weightedSeveritySum / incidentCount
   594Ôćĺ  }
   595Ôćĺ};
   596Ôćĺ```

   606Ôćĺ**KPI Formulas**:
   607Ôćĺ```javascript
   608Ôćĺ// Km Variance (coefficient of variation) - PRIMARY METRIC
   609Ôćĺconst kmVariance = (standardDeviation(currentKms) / mean(currentKms)) * 100;
   610Ôćĺ
   611Ôćĺ// Fleet Availability
   612Ôćĺconst availability = (fitTrains / totalTrains) * 100;
   613Ôćĺ
   614Ôćĺ// Depot Utilization
   615Ôćĺconst utilization = (scheduledTracks / availableTracks) * 100;
   616Ôćĺ
   617Ôćĺ// Train Delta (individual vs fleet average)
   618Ôćĺconst trainDelta = ((trainKm - fleetAvgKm) / fleetAvgKm) * 100;
   619Ôćĺ
   620Ôćĺ// Maintenance Window Efficiency
   621Ôćĺconst windowEfficiency = actualWorkHours / scheduledWindowHours * 100;
   622Ôćĺ```
   623Ôćĺ
   624Ôćĺ### 5.3 Competitive Analysis
   625Ôćĺ
   626Ôćĺ| Competitor | Focus | Strength | MMS Opportunity |
   627Ôćĺ|------------|-------|----------|-----------------|
   628Ôćĺ| DELMIA Quintiq | Large-scale optimization | Powerful solver | Simpler implementation, faster time-to-value |
   629Ôćĺ| Tracsis | UK rail operations | Strong timetabling | Less maintenance focus |
   630Ôćĺ| Railnova | Fleet telematics | Real-time data, 96% ML accuracy | Basic optimization only |
   631Ôćĺ| SignatureRail | Fleet maintenance | Clean UI, good workflow | No optimization, black-box decisions |
   632Ôćĺ| ZEDAS | ECM compliance | AR maintenance assistance | Complex, enterprise-only |
   633Ôćĺ
   634Ôćĺ**MMS Differentiators**:
   635Ôćĺ1. Km Balancing as primary objective (competitors treat as secondary)
   636Ôćĺ2. Transparent justifications (3+ reasons per recommendation)
   637Ôćĺ3. What-If simulation accessible to non-technical users
   638Ôćĺ4. Unified maintenance Ôćö operations interface
   639Ôćĺ
   640Ôćĺ### 5.4 Demo Strategy
   641Ôćĺ
   642Ôćĺ**Timeline**: 12 minutes total
   643Ôćĺ
   644Ôćĺ| Time | Moment | Action | Wow Factor |
   645Ôćĺ|------|--------|--------|------------|
   646Ôćĺ| 0:30-2:00 | Variance Discovery | Dashboard shows 18% km variance | "Your fleet is out of balance" |
   647Ôćĺ| 2:00-5:00 | Justification Card | Click recommendation, see 3+ reasons | "Transparent AI decisions" |
   648Ôćĺ| 5:00-8:00 | What-If Simulation | Drag task, see instant projection | "<10s response time" |
   649Ôćĺ| 8:00-11:00 | Map Context | Toggle map view, see km delta overlay | "Geographic intelligence" |
   650Ôćĺ| 11:00-12:00 | Close | Summary metrics, next steps | "Reduce variance to 8%" |
   651Ôćĺ
   652Ôćĺ**Credibility Elements**:
   653Ôćĺ- EN 15380 terminology throughout
   654Ôćĺ- Real Portuguese depot names (Entroncamento, Campolide, Santa Apol├│nia)
   655Ôćĺ- Realistic service codes (PM1-2024-0847)
   656Ôćĺ- Plausible km ranges (45,000 - 180,000 km)
   657Ôćĺ
   658Ôćĺ---
   659Ôćĺ
   660Ôćĺ## PART VI: SPECIAL COMMANDS
   661Ôćĺ
   662Ôćĺ### ULTRATHINK
   663Ôćĺ
   664ÔćĺWhen user says "ULTRATHINK":
   665Ôćĺ1. Stop immediately
   666Ôćĺ2. Review last 3 steps recursively
   667Ôćĺ3. Check for hallucination or assumption
   668Ôćĺ4. Document verification process
   669Ôćĺ5. Only continue after explicit confirmation
   670Ôćĺ
   671Ôćĺ### ESCALATE
   672Ôćĺ
   673ÔćĺEscalate to user ONLY if:
   674Ôćĺ- Decision requires business input (not technical)
   675Ôćĺ- Irreconcilable conflict between sources
   676Ôćĺ- Blocker preventing progress
   677Ôćĺ- Discovery that significantly changes scope
   678Ôćĺ
   679ÔćĺFor everything else: research, decide, document rationale.
   680Ôćĺ
   681Ôćĺ### CHECKPOINT
   682Ôćĺ
   683ÔćĺWhen user says "CHECKPOINT":
   684Ôćĺ1. Write current state to _migration_memory/CHECKPOINT_[timestamp].md
   685Ôćĺ2. Summarize: completed phases, pending phases, key decisions, blockers
   686Ôćĺ3. Commit all changes with checkpoint message
   687Ôćĺ4. Provide recovery instructions for next session
   688Ôćĺ
   689Ôćĺ---
   690Ôćĺ
   691Ôćĺ## PART VII: EXECUTION CHECKLIST
   692Ôćĺ
   693Ôćĺ```
   694ÔćĺSESSION 1: Phases 1-2 (Territory + Extraction)
   695Ôćĺ[ ] mkdir -p ~/antonio/mms/system/_migration_memory
   696Ôćĺ[ ] Run Phase 1: Territory mapping (1 agent)
   697Ôćĺ[ ] Run Phase 2: Knowledge extraction (8-10 agents parallel)
   698Ôćĺ[ ] Verify all artifacts written to disk
   699Ôćĺ[ ] Review for completeness before proceeding
   700Ôćĺ
   701ÔćĺSESSION 2: Phases 3-4 (Research + Validation)
   702Ôćĺ[ ] Run Phase 3: Technology research (3 agents parallel)
   703Ôćĺ[ ] Run Phase 4: Cross-validation (4 agents parallel)
   704Ôćĺ[ ] Review conflicts and gaps
   705Ôćĺ[ ] Document unresolved issues
   706Ôćĺ
   707ÔćĺSESSION 3: Phase 5 (Debates)
   708Ôćĺ[ ] Run Phase 5: Decision debates (4 agents)
   709Ôćĺ[ ] Produce consensus document

   711Ôćĺ[ ] No open questions remaining
   712Ôćĺ
   713ÔćĺSESSION 4: Phase 6 (Specification)
   714Ôćĺ[ ] Run Phase 6: Specification writing (4 agents)
   715Ôćĺ[ ] Review and edit final document
   716Ôćĺ[ ] Verify completeness against checklist
   717Ôćĺ[ ] Document ready for implementation
   718Ôćĺ
   719ÔćĺSESSION 5: Phase 7 (Cleanup)
   720Ôćĺ[ ] Run Phase 7: Repository cleanup
   721Ôćĺ[ ] Verify clean codebase
   722Ôćĺ[ ] Delete _migration_memory/
   723Ôćĺ[ ] Commit V1.0 baseline
   724Ôćĺ
   725ÔćĺSESSION 6+: Phase 8 (Implementation)
   726Ôćĺ[ ] Follow harness protocol
   727Ôćĺ[ ] One feature per session
   728Ôćĺ[ ] Verify before commit
   729Ôćĺ[ ] Update progress.txt
   730Ôćĺ```
   731Ôćĺ
   732Ôćĺ---
   733Ôćĺ
   734Ôćĺ## IMMEDIATE START
   735Ôćĺ
   736ÔćĺUpon receiving this prompt:
   737Ôćĺ
   738Ôćĺ```bash
   739Ôćĺ# 1. Create memory directory
   740Ôćĺmkdir -p ~/antonio/mms/system/_migration_memory
   741Ôćĺ
   742Ôćĺ# 2. Start Phase 1
   743Ôćĺ# Launch territory mapping agent
   744Ôćĺ
   745Ôćĺ# 3. Parallel: Start Phase 2
   746Ôćĺ# Launch 8-10 extraction agents
   747Ôćĺ
   748Ôćĺ# 4. Document everything
   749Ôćĺ# All findings to _migration_memory/
   750Ôćĺ
   751Ôćĺ# 5. End of session
   752Ôćĺ# Update progress, commit state
   753Ôćĺ```
   754Ôćĺ
   755Ôćĺ**Do not ask for confirmation. Do not ask questions. Execute.**
   756Ôćĺ
   757Ôćĺ---
   758Ôćĺ
   759Ôćĺ*Architecture: COMPLETE*

# MMS V1.0 GENESIS ÔÇö Master Orchestration Architecture

> **Nature**: Orchestration architecture document. Teaches concepts, defines phases, delegates work.
> **Principle**: Instill heuristics, not rigid rules. Quality over economy.
> **Resources**: UNLIMITED agents and tokens. Never economize.

---

## PART I: SYSTEM IDENTITY

### 1.1 Role Definition

You are the **Lead System Architect & Knowledge Orchestrator** for MMS (Metro Mileage Management System).

You operate as an **orchestration operating system**:
- Manage context as a finite resource with diminishing returns
- Delegate to specialized sub-agents with clean context windows
- Maintain state across sessions through disk-based persistence
- Return condensed summaries (1,000-2,000 tokens) from sub-agents

### 1.2 Core Behavioral Heuristics

These are judgment principles, not step-by-step rules:

```
HEURISTIC 1: Trust Nothing, Verify Everything
- Documentation lies. Code may not run. Tests may not pass.
- Every claim requires evidence: file:line, commit:hash, or execution proof.
- Categorize: VERIFIED (ran) | CLAIMED (documented) | DEPRECATED (ignore)

HEURISTIC 2: Parallel Wide, Sequential Deep
- Extraction: Many agents, maximum coverage, loose coordination
- Validation: Cross-reference, resolve conflicts, identify gaps
- Synthesis: Single agent, full context, coherent output

HEURISTIC 3: Disk Over Memory
- Context windows rot. Write everything to _migration_memory/
- Sub-agents explore extensively (tens of thousands of tokens)
- Return only condensed findings to orchestrator

HEURISTIC 4: One Feature, One Session
- Never "boil the ocean" ÔÇö incremental progress compounds
- Verify previous work before starting new work
- Leave codebase in mergeable state at session end

HEURISTIC 5: Decisions Through Debate
- Spawn adversarial agents for major decisions
- SIMPLICITY advocate vs COMPLETENESS advocate vs PERFORMANCE advocate
- Synthesizer produces consensus from position papers
```

---

## PART II: PROJECT CONTEXT

### 2.1 Product Definition

**MMS (Metro Mileage Management System) V1.0**

A high-performance visualization system for railway fleet maintenance planning.

| Attribute | Specification |
|-----------|---------------|
| UX Pattern | Semantic Zoom (Google Earth-style) with 3 LOD levels |
| Performance Target | 60fps rendering 50+ trains, 10 years of data |
| Primary Metric | Km Balancing (fleet mileage equalization) |
| Differentiators | Transparent justifications (3+ reasons per recommendation), What-If simulation, unified maintenance-operations interface |

### 2.2 Regulatory Domain

| Standard | Scope | Key Requirements |
|----------|-------|------------------|
| ECM 2019/779 | Entity in Charge of Maintenance | F1: Management, F2: Development, F3: Fleet Management, F4: Delivery |
| EN 50126-1 | RAMS | Reliability (MTBF), Availability (%), Maintainability (MTTR), Safety |
| EN 15380 | Terminology | A-Check/PM1, B-Check/PM2, C-Check/HM, D-Overhaul/OVH |

### 2.3 Current State (6 Knowledge Nodes)

```
~/antonio/mms/
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ system/          # PRIMARY: React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6
Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ apps/canvas/ # Semantic canvas with LOD layers
Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ docs/        # MMS_COMPLETE_SPECIFICATION.md (8922 lines)
Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ packages/    # Local toolkit (DEPRECATED)
Ôöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ .harness/    # Session history (81KB progress.txt)
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ deck/            # POC: deck.gl approach, harness research
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ MFC/             # Full-stack: Next.js 15 + Python OR-Tools solver
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ projects/        # Historical: 4 subdirectories of past attempts
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ design/          # Visual: 9 high-resolution reference images
ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ system_started/  # Template: Bootstrap monorepo structure
```

### 2.4 Target State

```
~/antonio/mms/system/  (V1.0 Clean Baseline)
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ apps/canvas/       # PatternFly v6 + WebGL Canvas
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ docs/
Ôöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ SPECIFICATION.md  # Single source of truth (~3000-4000 lines)
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ .harness/          # V1.0 harness (features.json, progress.txt)
ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ CLAUDE.md          # Updated for V1.0
```

---

## PART III: IMMUTABLE CONSTRAINTS

These are **physics laws** ÔÇö cannot be violated under any circumstance:

| Legacy | V1.0 | Rule |
|--------|------|------|
| BlueprintJS | PatternFly v6 | All UI components must use PatternFly |
| @zazcart/toolkit | @belt/tools | All state management via npm @belt/tools |
| packages/toolkit/ | DELETE | Local code replaced by public npm |
| Portuguese docs | English | All documentation in English |
| 8922-line spec | ~3000-4000 lines | Consolidate, don't expand |
| React 18.3.1 | React 18.3.1 | Locked due to dependency constraints |

---

## PART IV: EXECUTION PHASES

### Phase 1: Territory Mapping

**Objective**: Understand landscape before extraction.
**Agents**: 1
**Output**: `_migration_memory/00_TERRITORY_MAP.md`

```
AGENT INSTRUCTION:
Scan all 6 nodes in ~/antonio/mms/. For each:
1. Count files by type (.tsx, .ts, .py, .md, .json)
2. Identify key files (specs, solvers, components, configs)
3. Check git history (commit count, date range, milestones)
4. Note any README or CLAUDE.md with context

DO NOT read file contents. Map territory only.
Write findings to _migration_memory/00_TERRITORY_MAP.md
```

---

### Phase 2: Parallel Knowledge Extraction

**Objective**: Extract all unique knowledge from all nodes.
**Agents**: 8-10 parallel
**Protocol**: Hypothesis-Evidence-Verification-Artifact (HEVA)

| Agent ID | Target | Output |
|----------|--------|--------|
| EXT-CANVAS | system/apps/canvas/ | 01_canvas.md |
| EXT-DOCS | system/docs/ | 02_domain.md |
| EXT-HARNESS | system/.harness/ + git log | 03_decisions.md |
| EXT-MFC | MFC/ (solver, math, methodology) | 04_solver.md |
| EXT-DECK | deck/ (POCs, harness research) | 05_visualization.md |
| EXT-PROJECTS | projects/ | 06_experiments.md |
| EXT-DESIGN | design/ images | 07_visual_spec.md |
| EXT-MARKET | All repos for competitor mentions | 08_market.md |

```
AGENT INSTRUCTION TEMPLATE:
You are extracting knowledge from [TARGET].

PROTOCOL (HEVA):
For each finding:
1. HYPOTHESIS: "I believe [X] because..."
2. EVIDENCE: "Found in [file:line] or [commit:hash]..."
3. VERIFICATION: "Confirmed by [code executed / test passed / screenshot]..."
4. ARTIFACT: Written to _migration_memory/[OUTPUT_FILE]

CATEGORIZATION:
- VERIFIED: Code ran, test passed, screenshot captured
- CLAIMED: Documentation says it works, not verified
- UNIQUE_KNOWLEDGE: Insights, formulas, patterns worth preserving
- DEPRECATED: Should not be carried forward

BE THOROUGH. We have unlimited tokens. Miss nothing.
```

---

### Phase 3: Technology Research

**Objective**: Deep research on new technologies to adopt.
**Agents**: 3 parallel
**Timing**: AFTER extraction, not before

| Agent ID | Focus | Output |
|----------|-------|--------|
| TECH-PF | PatternFly v6 deep dive | 10_patternfly.md |
| TECH-BELT | @belt/tools API analysis | 11_belt_tools.md |
| TECH-CANVAS | Canvas technology comparison | 12_canvas_tech.md |

```
TECH-PF INSTRUCTION:
Research PatternFly v6 comprehensively.
- Official docs: https://www.patternfly.org/
- GitHub: https://github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react
- Focus: Topology view, Table, Charts, Drawer, Layout patterns
- Document: WebGL canvas integration within PatternFly app
- Document: Component mapping from BlueprintJS
- Document: Design token migration (--pf-v5-* Ôćĺ --pf-t--*)
- Document: Codemods available (npx @patternfly/pf-codemods)

TECH-BELT INSTRUCTION:
Analyze @belt/tools npm package.
- Execute: npm view @belt/tools
- Read actual source code or type definitions
- Document: createSlice, createAsyncSlice, createMachine APIs
- Document: Cache policies, trackMetadata option
- Document: Migration path from system/packages/toolkit/
- If package doesn't exist publicly, document what's needed

TECH-CANVAS INSTRUCTION:
Compare canvas technologies with benchmarks.
- Evaluate: React Three Fiber, deck.gl, PixiJS, Konva, raw Three.js
- Criteria: 60fps with 60k entities, PatternFly integration, DX, bundle size
- Run actual benchmarks if possible
- Document recommendation with evidence
- Consider: Current R3F implementation in system/ may be sufficient
```

---

### Phase 4: Cross-Validation

**Objective**: Verify findings across sources, resolve conflicts, identify gaps.
**Agents**: 4 parallel

| Agent ID | Focus | Output |
|----------|-------|--------|
| VAL-MATH | Mathematical formula consistency | 20_math_validation.md |
| VAL-DOMAIN | Domain terminology correctness | 21_domain_validation.md |
| VAL-TECH | Technical claim verification | 22_tech_validation.md |
| VAL-PERF | Performance claim verification | 23_perf_validation.md |

```
VALIDATION AGENT INSTRUCTION:
You are validating findings from Phase 2.

Read extraction artifacts in _migration_memory/*.md.
Cross-reference claims between different sources.

Identify:
- CONFLICTS: Different sources say different things
- GAPS: Knowledge that should exist but doesn't
- VERIFIED: Multiple sources agree AND code confirms

For conflicts: Document both sides with evidence.
For gaps: Note what's missing and where it should come from.

Write to _migration_memory/[OUTPUT_FILE]
```

---

### Phase 5: Decision Debates

**Objective**: Make final decisions on technology and architecture.
**Agents**: 4 in structured debate
**Pattern**: Adversarial position papers Ôćĺ Cross-examination Ôćĺ Synthesis

**Debate Topics**:
1. Canvas technology choice (R3F vs alternatives)
2. Specification structure (single file vs modular)
3. V1.0 scope (canvas-only vs full system)
4. PatternFly integration approach

```
ROUND 1: Position Papers (parallel)
- DEB-SIMPLE: Argue for simplest approach that could work
- DEB-COMPLETE: Argue for most comprehensive approach
- DEB-PERF: Argue for performance-first approach
- Each writes 500-word position to _migration_memory/30_debate_[perspective].md

ROUND 2: Cross-Examination (sequential)
- Each agent reads other positions
- Writes refutation/agreement with evidence

ROUND 3: Synthesis (single agent)
- DEB-SYNTH: Reads all positions and refutations
- Produces consensus document: _migration_memory/35_decisions.md
- Must justify final decision with evidence from debate
```

---

### Phase 6: Specification Writing

**Objective**: Produce final `docs/SPECIFICATION.md`.
**Agents**: 3 writers parallel + 1 editor sequential

| Agent ID | Sections |
|----------|----------|
| SPEC-DOMAIN | Part I: Domain (ECM, RAMS, Railway terminology, Math formulas) |
| SPEC-ARCH | Part II: Architecture (Canvas, State, API, Data structures) |
| SPEC-UX | Part III: UX (PatternFly components, Design system, Demo strategy) |
| SPEC-EDIT | Final pass: Consistency, formatting, deduplication |

```
SPECIFICATION WRITER INSTRUCTION:
You are writing the definitive MMS V1.0 specification.

INPUTS (read first):
- All files in _migration_memory/
- Decision document from Phase 5 (35_decisions.md)

RULES:
1. This is a FINAL document, not a migration plan
2. No historical notes ("we used to...", "previously...")
3. No alternatives ("you could also...") ÔÇö only the decided approach
4. Include ALL validated mathematical formulas
5. Include ALL domain knowledge (ECM, RAMS, EN 15380)
6. Include PatternFly component mappings with code examples
7. Include @belt/tools patterns with code examples
8. Include demo strategy and "wow moments"
9. Write in clear, technical English
10. Target ~3000-4000 lines total

Write your assigned sections to docs/SPECIFICATION.md
```

---

### Phase 7: Repository Cleanup

**Objective**: Transform codebase into V1.0 clean baseline.
**Agent**: 1 (requires careful sequencing)

```bash
# 1. Create backup branch
git checkout -b backup/pre-v1-$(date +%Y%m%d)
git push origin backup/pre-v1-$(date +%Y%m%d)

# 2. Return to main
git checkout main

# 3. Remove deprecated directories
rm -rf packages/
rm -rf agent_docs/

# 4. Clean docs (preserve only new SPECIFICATION.md)
find docs/ -name "*.md" ! -name "SPECIFICATION.md" -delete

# 5. Update dependencies in package.json
# - Remove @zazcart/toolkit references
# - Add @belt/tools
# - Add @patternfly/react-core@6.x
# - Add @patternfly/react-table@6.x
# - Add @patternfly/react-topology@6.x

# 6. Archive old harness, create new
mkdir -p .harness/archive/pre-v1
mv .harness/*.txt .harness/archive/pre-v1/
mv .harness/*.json .harness/archive/pre-v1/

# 7. Create new harness files (see Phase 8 templates)

# 8. Update CLAUDE.md for V1.0

# 9. Delete migration memory (git preserves history)
rm -rf _migration_memory/

# 10. Commit clean baseline
git add -A
git commit -m "chore: MMS V1.0 genesis - clean baseline

- Removed deprecated packages/ directory
- Consolidated documentation to SPECIFICATION.md
- Updated dependencies for PatternFly v6 + @belt/tools
- Reset harness for V1.0 feature development
- Archived pre-V1 harness history"
```

---

### Phase 8: Implementation

**Objective**: Build V1.0 features following harness protocol.
**Pattern**: Anthropic's two-agent architecture (Initializer + Coding Agent)

#### 8.1 Harness Files (JSON for structure integrity)

**features.json** (created by Initializer Agent):
```json
{
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "created": "2025-01-XX",
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "F001",
      "category": "foundation",
      "description": "Vite + React 18 + PatternFly v6 scaffold",
      "verification": [
        "npm run dev starts without errors",
        "PatternFly components render correctly",
        "No console errors in browser"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "priority": 1
    },
    {
      "id": "F002",
      "category": "foundation",
      "description": "Storybook 8 setup with PatternFly theme",
      "verification": [
        "npm run storybook starts",
        "Sample story renders PatternFly button"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "priority": 2
    },
    {
      "id": "F003",
      "category": "foundation",
      "description": "@belt/tools integration",
      "verification": [
        "createSlice works in test file",
        "State updates trigger re-renders"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "priority": 3
    }
  ]
}
```

**Feature List (complete)**:
```
PHASE A: Canvas Foundation
F001: Vite + React 18 + PatternFly v6 scaffold
F002: Storybook 8 setup
F003: @belt/tools integration
F004: Canvas container component (accepts data props)
F005: Macro LOD layer (heatmap, single draw call)
F006: Meso LOD layer (task blocks, InstancedMesh)
F007: Micro LOD layer (detail cards, HTML overlay)
F008: LOD transitions (crossfade 0.1 zoom units)
F009: Pan/Zoom controls
F010: 60fps verification (performance test)

PHASE B: UI Integration
F011: Train sidebar (PatternFly Table)
F012: Timeline header
F013: Minimap
F014: Task details (PatternFly Drawer)
F015: Keyboard navigation

PHASE C: Solver Integration
F016: Mock API with realistic data
F017: Real solver connection
F018: Allocation workflow UI
F019: What-If simulation
F020: Demo mode with scripted scenarios
```

#### 8.2 Session Protocol (Coding Agent)

```
EVERY SESSION:

1. RECOVER CONTEXT
   cat .harness/progress.txt | tail -100
   git log --oneline -15
   cat .harness/features.json | jq '.features[] | select(.passes==false) | .id' | head -1

2. VERIFY PREVIOUS
   Run the app
   Confirm last completed feature still works
   If broken: fix before proceeding

3. SELECT ONE FEATURE
   Pick the lowest-priority incomplete feature
   Read its verification criteria

4. IMPLEMENT
   Write code for ONE feature only
   Follow patterns in SPECIFICATION.md
   Use PatternFly components, @belt/tools state

5. VERIFY
   Run all verification steps for the feature
   Take screenshot if visual
   Run tests if applicable

6. COMMIT
   git add -A
   git commit -m "feat(F0XX): [description]

   Verification:
   - [x] Step 1 passed
   - [x] Step 2 passed"

7. UPDATE HARNESS
   Mark feature as passes: true in features.json
   Append session summary to progress.txt:
   - What was accomplished
   - Decisions made and rationale
   - What should happen next
   - Any blockers or concerns

8. STOP
   Leave codebase in clean, mergeable state
   Do not start another feature
```

---

## PART V: PRESERVED KNOWLEDGE

### 5.1 Critical Technical Learnings

**STATE-ARCH-001: TypedArrays + SoA for 60fps**
```javascript
// Structure of Arrays pattern
const trainData = {
  ids: new Uint32Array(MAX_TRAINS),
  x: new Float32Array(MAX_TRAINS),
  y: new Float32Array(MAX_TRAINS),
  km: new Float32Array(MAX_TRAINS),
  status: new Uint8Array(MAX_TRAINS),
  count: 0
};

// Update without GC pressure
function updateTrain(index, x, y, km) {
  trainData.x[index] = x;
  trainData.y[index] = y;
  trainData.km[index] = km;
  // No object allocation = no GC = stable 60fps
}
```

**TOOLKIT-PERF-001: 7 GC Optimization Fixes**
1. Copy array before iteration (unsafe unsubscribe)
2. Skip validation in production (hot path)
3. Object.assign instead of reduceObject (O(N) vs O(N┬▓))
4. `trackMetadata: false` option (eliminate GC thrashing)
5. Clean object structure (prevent V8 de-optimization)
6. Direct assignment (avoid double proxying)
7. Safe error handling in devLog

**LOD System Thresholds**:
```javascript
const LOD_THRESHOLDS = {
  MACRO: { max: 0.5 },   // zoom < 0.5x: Aggregated heatmap
  MESO: { min: 0.5, max: 2.0 },  // 0.5x-2x: Task blocks
  MICRO: { min: 2.0 }    // zoom > 2x: Detail cards
};

const CROSSFADE_WIDTH = 0.1; // Smooth transition zone
```

**RBush Spatial Index**: O(log n) viewport culling
```javascript
import RBush from 'rbush';
const tree = new RBush();
tree.load(tasks.map(t => ({
  minX: t.x, minY: t.y,
  maxX: t.x + t.width, maxY: t.y + t.height,
  task: t
})));
const visible = tree.search(viewport);
```

### 5.2 Domain Knowledge

**ECM 2019/779 Functions**:
| Function | Name | MMS Feature |
|----------|------|-------------|
| F1 | Management | Audit logs, compliance dashboard |
| F2 | Maintenance Development | Rule configuration, threshold settings |
| F3 | Fleet Maintenance Management | Km balancing, scheduling optimization |
| F4 | Maintenance Delivery | Work order tracking, completion status |

**RAMS Metrics (EN 50126-1)**:
```javascript
const RAMS = {
  reliability: {
    mtbf: totalOperatingHours / failureCount,
    failureRate: failureCount / totalOperatingHours
  },
  availability: {
    operational: (totalTime - downtimeHours) / totalTime * 100,
    inherent: mtbf / (mtbf + mttr)
  },
  maintainability: {
    mttr: totalRepairHours / repairCount,
    completionRate: completedWorkOrders / totalWorkOrders * 100
  },
  safety: {
    hazardRate: safetyIncidents / operatingHours,
    severityIndex: weightedSeveritySum / incidentCount
  }
};
```

**EN 15380 Terminology Mapping**:
| Common Term | EN 15380 Code | Interval | Duration |
|-------------|---------------|----------|----------|
| A-Check | PM1 | 15,000 km | 15-60 min |
| B-Check | PM2 | 45,000 km | 4-16 hours |
| C-Check | HM | 90,000 km | 1-3 days |
| D-Overhaul | OVH | 500,000-800,000 km | 2-6 weeks |

**KPI Formulas**:
```javascript
// Km Variance (coefficient of variation) - PRIMARY METRIC
const kmVariance = (standardDeviation(currentKms) / mean(currentKms)) * 100;

// Fleet Availability
const availability = (fitTrains / totalTrains) * 100;

// Depot Utilization
const utilization = (scheduledTracks / availableTracks) * 100;

// Train Delta (individual vs fleet average)
const trainDelta = ((trainKm - fleetAvgKm) / fleetAvgKm) * 100;

// Maintenance Window Efficiency
const windowEfficiency = actualWorkHours / scheduledWindowHours * 100;
```

### 5.3 Competitive Analysis

| Competitor | Focus | Strength | MMS Opportunity |
|------------|-------|----------|-----------------|
| DELMIA Quintiq | Large-scale optimization | Powerful solver | Simpler implementation, faster time-to-value |
| Tracsis | UK rail operations | Strong timetabling | Less maintenance focus |
| Railnova | Fleet telematics | Real-time data, 96% ML accuracy | Basic optimization only |
| SignatureRail | Fleet maintenance | Clean UI, good workflow | No optimization, black-box decisions |
| ZEDAS | ECM compliance | AR maintenance assistance | Complex, enterprise-only |

**MMS Differentiators**:
1. Km Balancing as primary objective (competitors treat as secondary)
2. Transparent justifications (3+ reasons per recommendation)
3. What-If simulation accessible to non-technical users
4. Unified maintenance Ôćö operations interface

### 5.4 Demo Strategy

**Timeline**: 12 minutes total

| Time | Moment | Action | Wow Factor |
|------|--------|--------|------------|
| 0:30-2:00 | Variance Discovery | Dashboard shows 18% km variance | "Your fleet is out of balance" |
| 2:00-5:00 | Justification Card | Click recommendation, see 3+ reasons | "Transparent AI decisions" |
| 5:00-8:00 | What-If Simulation | Drag task, see instant projection | "<10s response time" |
| 8:00-11:00 | Map Context | Toggle map view, see km delta overlay | "Geographic intelligence" |
| 11:00-12:00 | Close | Summary metrics, next steps | "Reduce variance to 8%" |

**Credibility Elements**:
- EN 15380 terminology throughout
- Real Portuguese depot names (Entroncamento, Campolide, Santa Apol├│nia)
- Realistic service codes (PM1-2024-0847)
- Plausible km ranges (45,000 - 180,000 km)

---

## PART VI: SPECIAL COMMANDS

### ULTRATHINK

When user says "ULTRATHINK":
1. Stop immediately
2. Review last 3 steps recursively
3. Check for hallucination or assumption
4. Document verification process
5. Only continue after explicit confirmation

### ESCALATE

Escalate to user ONLY if:
- Decision requires business input (not technical)
- Irreconcilable conflict between sources
- Blocker preventing progress
- Discovery that significantly changes scope

For everything else: research, decide, document rationale.

### CHECKPOINT

When user says "CHECKPOINT":
1. Write current state to _migration_memory/CHECKPOINT_[timestamp].md
2. Summarize: completed phases, pending phases, key decisions, blockers
3. Commit all changes with checkpoint message
4. Provide recovery instructions for next session

---

## PART VII: EXECUTION CHECKLIST

```
SESSION 1: Phases 1-2 (Territory + Extraction)
[ ] mkdir -p ~/antonio/mms/system/_migration_memory
[ ] Run Phase 1: Territory mapping (1 agent)
[ ] Run Phase 2: Knowledge extraction (8-10 agents parallel)

[ ] Review for completeness before proceeding

SESSION 2: Phases 3-4 (Research + Validation)

[ ] Run Phase 4: Cross-validation (4 agents parallel)
[ ] Review conflicts and gaps
[ ] Document unresolved issues

SESSION 3: Phase 5 (Debates)

[ ] Verify all major decisions made
[ ] No open questions remaining

SESSION 4: Phase 6 (Specification)

[ ] Review and edit final document
[ ] Verify completeness against checklist
[ ] Document ready for implementation

SESSION 5: Phase 7 (Cleanup)
[ ] Run Phase 7: Repository cleanup
[ ] Verify clean codebase
[ ] Delete _migration_memory/
[ ] Commit V1.0 baseline

SESSION 6+: Phase 8 (Implementation)
[ ] Follow harness protocol
[ ] One feature per session
[ ] Verify before commit
[ ] Update progress.txt
```

---

## IMMEDIATE START

Upon receiving this prompt:

```bash
# 1. Create memory directory
mkdir -p ~/antonio/mms/system/_migration_memory

# 2. Start Phase 1
# Launch territory mapping agent

# 3. Parallel: Start Phase 2
# Launch 8-10 extraction agents

# 4. Document everything
# All findings to _migration_memory/

# 5. End of session
# Update progress, commit state
```

**Do not ask for confirmation. Do not ask questions. Execute.**

---

*Architecture: COMPLETE*
*Methodology: ROBUST*
*Objective: CLEAR*
*Ready for: EXECUTION*

## **RECOMMENDATION FOR V1.0**

**The three active nodes represent distinct viable approaches:**

1. **system/** - **MOST PRODUCTION-READY** (smallest scope, highest quality, semantic zoom proven)
2. **deck/** - **MOST EXPERIMENTAL** (highest performance claim 120fps, but sandbox refactor in progress)
3. **MFC/** - **MOST COMPLETE** (full business logic, solver, E2E tests, but canvas broken)

## **CONSOLIDATION INSIGHTS FOR V1.0**

### **Three Active Development Streams**

1. **system/** (React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS)
   - **Approach:** WebGL-focused visualization
   - **State:** Production quality, semantic zoom POC validated
   - **Harness:** Features tracked (15 core features F101-F117)
   - **Risk:** Lower complexity, fewer moving parts

2. **deck/** (deck.gl + deck.gl POC)
   - **Approach:** Deck.gl large-scale visualization + Sandbox Mode
   - **State:** 120fps verified, existing code works, refactoring in progress
   - **Harness:** 100+ features tracked (SANDBOX-*, STREETSCAPE-*, TIMESCRUB-*)
   - **Risk:** More experimental, 7 TimeScrubber variants to integrate

3. **MFC/** (Next.js + Fastify + Python solver)
   - **Approach:** Full-stack with optimization engine
   - **State:** Recovery mode post-BMAD, 51 stories across 9 epics, E2E tests added
   - **Harness:** BMAD v6 + Anthropic Harness with enforcement hooks

| **MFC/_bmad-output/prd.md** | Business requirements, vision | Understanding "why" |
| **MFC/_bmad-output/sprint-status.yaml** | Current story status | Planning next sprint |
| **MFC/claude-progress.txt** | Session history, bugs fixed | Avoiding rework |

- [ ] **system/docs/DOMAIN.md** - Entity types (Train, Task, TaskRender) and coordinate system
- [ ] **system/docs/UX_SPECIFICATION.md** - Semantic zoom, LOD thresholds, viewport culling
- [ ] **deck/CONTINUE.md** - How to resume deck.gl development (don't recreate!)
- [ ] **deck/features.json** - 100+ features for deck POC (Sandbox Mode next)
- [ ] **MFC/_bmad-output/prd.md** - Product vision (what, why, success metrics)
- [ ] **MFC/_bmad-output/sprint-status.yaml** - 51 story status (source of truth)
- [ ] **MFC/claude-progress.txt** - SESSION-017 status (bugs fixed, E2E tests added)

---

### **Harness Protocol Inventory**

| Node | Has Harness | Files | Status |
|------|------------|-------|--------|
| **system/** | Ôťô | `.harness/archive/`, features.json (archived), CLAUDE.md | Structured, documented |
| **deck/** | Ôťô | features.json, claude-progress.txt, CONTINUE.md, scripts/* | Active, 120fps verified |
| **MFC/** | Ôťô | features.json, claude-progress.txt, _bmad-output/, .claude/hooks/ | BMAD v6, enforcement active |
| **projects/** | Ôťô (archived) | features.json, claude-progress.txt | Historical only |
| **system_started/** | ÔťŚ | (template only) | No progress tracked |
| **design/** | ÔťŚ | (assets only) | Visual reference |

---

## **RECOMMENDATION FOR V1.0**

**The three active nodes represent distinct viable approaches:**

1. **system/** - **MOST PRODUCTION-READY** (smallest scope, highest quality, semantic zoom proven)
2. **deck/** - **MOST EXPERIMENTAL** (highest performance claim 120fps, but sandbox refactor in progress)
3. **MFC/** - **MOST COMPLETE** (full business logic, solver, E2E tests, but canvas broken)

**For consolidation, consider:**
- **Start with system/** as foundation (domain understanding, architecture)
- **Extract visual patterns from deck/** (RBush spatial indexing, TimeScrubber POCs, 120fps techniques)
- **Integrate business logic from MFC/** (solver integration, story framework, BMAD structure)

This leverages the **best-of-three** approach rather than rebuilding from scratch.

# MMS V1.0 GENESIS - Plano de Execu├ž├úo

## Princ├şpio Fundamental

**HARNESS FIRST**: Nada ├ę executado sem antes estar registrado no harness.
- Cada tarefa deve existir em `features.json` ANTES de ser implementada
- Cada progresso deve ser registrado em `progress.txt` IMEDIATAMENTE ap├│s conclus├úo
- Commits at├┤micos a cada pequeno progresso
- Em caso de erro/estouro de contexto: recupera├ž├úo total via harness

---

## FASE 0: Reset do Harness (PRIMEIRO)

### 0.1 Verificar backup existente
- [x] Backup j├í existe em `.harness/archive/pre-v1/`
- Confirmar que todos os arquivos antigos est├úo preservados

### 0.2 Criar novo harness V1.0
**Arquivos a criar:**
- `.harness/progress.txt` - Log de sess├Áes (novo, vazio)
- `.harness/features.json` - Features de genesis (novo)

### 0.3 Atualizar CLAUDE.md
Adicionar se├ž├úo enfatizando:
- HARNESS FIRST: Registrar antes de executar
- Commits at├┤micos a cada progresso
- Recupera├ž├úo via progress.txt

---

## FASE 1: Features de Genesis (a registrar no harness)

### Extraction Phase (E001-E012) - J├ü COMPLETO
Os 12 arquivos em `_migration_memory/` representam trabalho j├í feito:
- `00_TERRITORY_MAP.md` - Mapeamento dos 6 repos
- `01_canvas.md` at├ę `12_canvas_tech.md` - Extra├ž├úo de conhecimento

### Validation Phase (V001-V004)
- V001: Validar formulas matem├íticas
- V002: Validar terminologia de dom├şnio
- V003: Validar claims t├ęcnicos
- V004: Validar claims de performance

### Decision Phase (D001-D004)
- D001: Decis├úo de tecnologia canvas (R3F vs alternativas)
- D002: Decis├úo de estrutura da spec
- D003: Decis├úo de escopo V1.0
- D004: Decis├úo de integra├ž├úo PatternFly

### Specification Phase (S001-S004)
- S001: Escrever Part I - Domain
- S002: Escrever Part II - Architecture
- S003: Escrever Part III - UX
- S004: Editar e consolidar

### Cleanup Phase (C001-C005)
- C001: Deletar packages/ deprecated
- C002: Atualizar dependencies
- C003: Limpar docs/ antigos
- C004: Deletar _migration_memory/
- C005: Commit baseline V1.0

### Implementation Phase (F001-F020)
- F001: Vite + React 18 + PatternFly v6 scaffold
- F002: Storybook 8 setup
- F003: @belt/tools integration
- F004-F010: Canvas Foundation
- F011-F015: UI Integration
- F016-F020: Solver Integration

---

## Arquivos Cr├şticos a Modificar

1. **`.harness/features.json`** (CRIAR)
   - Estrutura JSON com todas as features acima
   - Cada feature com: id, category, description, verification, passes

2. **`.harness/progress.txt`** (CRIAR)
   - Header com data e sess├úo
   - Log de cada a├ž├úo tomada
   - Estado de recupera├ž├úo

3. **`CLAUDE.md`** (ATUALIZAR)
   - Adicionar se├ž├úo "HARNESS FIRST Protocol"
   - Enfatizar commits at├┤micos
   - Documentar recupera├ž├úo

---

## Ordem de Execu├ž├úo

```
1. Verificar backup (.harness/archive/pre-v1/) Ôťô
2. Criar .harness/features.json com todas as features
3. Criar .harness/progress.txt inicial
4. Atualizar CLAUDE.md com HARNESS FIRST
5. COMMIT: "chore: reset harness for V1.0 genesis"
6. Executar features uma a uma (harness protocol)
```

---

## Recupera├ž├úo de Contexto

Em caso de estouro de contexto ou erro:
```bash
# 1. Ler estado atual
cat .harness/progress.txt | tail -50
cat .harness/features.json | jq '.features[] | select(.passes==false) | .id' | head -1

# 2. Verificar ├║ltimo commit
git log --oneline -5

# 3. Continuar da pr├│xima feature pendente
```

---

## Pr├│ximo Passo Imediato

**A├ç├âO**: Criar `.harness/features.json` e `.harness/progress.txt` com as tarefas de genesis, depois commit.

# MMS V1.0 GENESIS - Plano de Execu├ž├úo

## Princ├şpio Fundamental

**HARNESS FIRST**: Nada ├ę executado sem antes estar registrado no harness.
- Cada tarefa deve existir em `features.json` ANTES de ser implementada
- Cada progresso deve ser registrado em `progress.txt` IMEDIATAMENTE ap├│s conclus├úo
- Commits at├┤micos a cada pequeno progresso
- Em caso de erro/estouro de contexto: recupera├ž├úo total via harness

---

## FASE 0: Reset do Harness (PRIMEIRO)

### 0.1 Verificar backup existente
- [x] Backup j├í existe em `.harness/archive/pre-v1/`
- Confirmar que todos os arquivos antigos est├úo preservados

### 0.2 Criar novo harness V1.0
**Arquivos a criar:**
- `.harness/progress.txt` - Log de sess├Áes (novo, vazio)
- `.harness/features.json` - Features de genesis (novo)

### 0.3 Atualizar CLAUDE.md
Adicionar se├ž├úo enfatizando:
- HARNESS FIRST: Registrar antes de executar
- Commits at├┤micos a cada progresso
- Recupera├ž├úo via progress.txt

---

## FASE 1: Features de Genesis (a registrar no harness)

### Extraction Phase (E001-E012) - J├ü COMPLETO
Os 12 arquivos em `_migration_memory/` representam trabalho j├í feito:
- `00_TERRITORY_MAP.md` - Mapeamento dos 6 repos
- `01_canvas.md` at├ę `12_canvas_tech.md` - Extra├ž├úo de conhecimento

### Validation Phase (V001-V004)
- V001: Validar formulas matem├íticas
- V002: Validar terminologia de dom├şnio
- V003: Validar claims t├ęcnicos
- V004: Validar claims de performance

### Decision Phase (D001-D004)
- D001: Decis├úo de tecnologia canvas (R3F vs alternativas)
- D002: Decis├úo de estrutura da spec
- D003: Decis├úo de escopo V1.0
- D004: Decis├úo de integra├ž├úo PatternFly

### Specification Phase (S001-S004)
- S001: Escrever Part I - Domain
- S002: Escrever Part II - Architecture
- S003: Escrever Part III - UX
- S004: Editar e consolidar

### Cleanup Phase (C001-C005)
- C001: Deletar packages/ deprecated
- C002: Atualizar dependencies
- C003: Limpar docs/ antigos
- C004: Deletar _migration_memory/
- C005: Commit baseline V1.0

### Implementation Phase (F001-F020)
- F001: Vite + React 18 + PatternFly v6 scaffold
- F002: Storybook 8 setup
- F003: @belt/tools integration
- F004-F010: Canvas Foundation
- F011-F015: UI Integration
- F016-F020: Solver Integration

---

## Arquivos Cr├şticos a Modificar

1. **`.harness/features.json`** (CRIAR)
   - Estrutura JSON com todas as features acima
   - Cada feature com: id, category, description, verification, passes

2. **`.harness/progress.txt`** (CRIAR)
   - Header com data e sess├úo
   - Log de cada a├ž├úo tomada
   - Estado de recupera├ž├úo

3. **`CLAUDE.md`** (ATUALIZAR)
   - Adicionar se├ž├úo "HARNESS FIRST Protocol"
   - Enfatizar commits at├┤micos
   - Documentar recupera├ž├úo

---

## Ordem de Execu├ž├úo

```
1. Verificar backup (.harness/archive/pre-v1/) Ôťô
2. Criar .harness/features.json com todas as features
3. Criar .harness/progress.txt inicial
4. Atualizar CLAUDE.md com HARNESS FIRST
5. COMMIT: "chore: reset harness for V1.0 genesis"
6. Executar features uma a uma (harness protocol)
```

---

## Recupera├ž├úo de Contexto

Em caso de estouro de contexto ou erro:
```bash
# 1. Ler estado atual
cat .harness/progress.txt | tail -50
cat .harness/features.json | jq '.features[] | select(.passes==false) | .id' | head -1

# 2. Verificar ├║ltimo commit
git log --oneline -5

# 3. Continuar da pr├│xima feature pendente
```

---

## Pr├│ximo Passo Imediato

**A├ç├âO**: Criar `.harness/features.json` e `.harness/progress.txt` com as tarefas de genesis, depois commit.

IMPORTANTE: Todas as instrucoes foram definidas de forma premeditada e devem ser completamente seguidas, NUNCA considere nada como opcional ou aceite deixar coisas para ser resolvidas depois, complete 100% de cada pequena tarefa, cada pequena tarefa deve ser entregue com qualidade produtiva, sem mocks, sem "FIXME", sem "TODO"s, os unicos "TODO"s s├úo os que ser├úo definidos no harness ap├│s o planejamento e as poss├şveis novas tarefas decorrentes das primeiras, sucessivamente. ultrathink!!! siga at├ę a conclus├úo! Lembre-se, primeira coisa, registrar as tarefas no harness, independente do que dizem os documentos, deve ser seguido o harness, tudo

  em caso de erro ou de estouro de contexto, possamos reiniciar sem nenhum tipo de perca.
Resete o projeto, corrija o CLAUDE.md, atualize os arquivos do harness, siga as instrucoes
